item.title: Jikji - 用于在 React 中轻松构建报告的布局库 | Jikji - Layout library for building effortless reports in React
item.description:
我有点痴迷于报告和 PDF 工具 :) 它始于一个简单的需求。 为我的项目创建 PDF 报告，有一些用于构建 PDF 的库，但其中大多数不支持非英语或从右到左的语言。 此外，与他们一起工作和设计报告也很困难。

我最终决定继续使用 HTML 到 PDF 的解决方案，因为它可以让我自由地设计我的报告，并且不存在本地化问题。 同样，大多数库不支持页眉/页脚或分页。 支持这些功能的工具是基于 QtWebKit 构建的，不支持新的 HTML/CSS 规范。

最后，他们都没有满足我的需求，所以我开发了一个工具，可以通过将页眉和页脚与内容分离并创建两个 HTML 文件来将 HTML 转换为 PDF。 将 HTML 转换为 PDF（通过 puppeteer）并最终将两个文件的每一页合并在一起。

这个 hacky 解决方案解决了我和其他开发人员的问题（根据 NPM，在我写这篇博客时它每周有 900 次下载）。

尽管如此，我还是在 GitHub 上收到了一堆关于新功能的请求，但不得不拒绝其中的大部分请求，因为puppeteer在布局和打印的自定义方面有很多限制。

当我在 Google Docs 中编写文档时，我产生了这个想法。 当光标跳转到新页面时，我问自己他们是如何处理分页的？ 它有一个示例答案。 在浏览器决定如何拆分/包装行和元素来布局页面之前，它们会通过 JS 中断行和元素。

所以我开始开发一个 JS 引擎，在浏览器打印预览引擎获取元素之前处理分页。 它让我可以编辑和自定义每个引擎对任何组件的行为。

结果是“Jikji”库（为什么是 Jikji？它需要另一个博客:)））。 它使用React Fiber获取组件层次结构，并在渲染和制作可轻松打印或转换为 PDF 或图像的可打印 Web 视图之前重新组织、拆分和布局组件。

我正在尝试完成文档并准备发布。 很快您就可以查看Jikji表格



原文:

I'm kinda obsessed with reports and PDFs tools :) It started with a simple need. Creating PDF reports for my project, There were a few libraries for building PDFs, but most of them didn't support non-English or right to left language. Also, it was tough to work and design reports with them.

I finally decided to continue with HTML to PDF solutions as it lets me design my report freely, and there was no localization problem. Again most of the libs don't support Header/Footer or pagination. The tools that support these features were built over QtWebKit and didn't support new HTML/CSS specifications.

In the end, none of them satisfied my needs, So I developed a tool that can convert HTML to PDF by separating the header and footer from content and creating two HTML files. Converting both HTML to PDF (by puppeteer) and eventually merging each page of two files together.

This hacky solution resolved my problems and other developers (According to the NPM, it has 900 weekly downloads at the time I'm writing this blog).

Github: puppeteer-report

puppeteer-report provides a simple solution for having a custom header, footer, and page numbers. Still, I got a bunch of requests on GitHub for new features and had to reject most of them because puppeteer has a lot of limitations in the customization of layout and printing.

"NOT POSSIBLE" Was not a satisfactory answer for me, so I started a new project to fix all of the limitations and provide more features. The idea came to me when I was working on a doc in Google Docs. When the cursor jumped to a new page, I asked myself how they handled the pagination? It has a sample answer. They break lines and elements by JS before the browser decides to how split/wrap the lines and elements to layout the pages.

So I started to develop a JS engine that handles the pagination before the browser print preview engine gets the elements. It lets me edit and customize every engine's behavior over any component.

The result was "Jikji" library (why Jikji? it will need another blog:)) ). It uses React Fiber to get the component hierarchy and reorganized, split, and layout the components before rendering and making a print-ready web view that can easily be printed or converted to PDF or images.

I'm trying to complete the documentation and make it ready to publish. soon you will be able to check form Jikji
item.author: pejmannik

item.title: 8 个实际上非常有用的未知 HTML 标签 | 8 Unknown HTML tags that are actually quite useful
item.description:
山姆-孙达

发表于被认为重要的 HTML 标签是常用的标签，但事实并非如此，有一些重要的 HTML 标签大多数人不使用并最终浪费时间编写等效的 CSS


让我们开始吧

文本将通过一条线显示 S 代表 Strike-Through 通常用于电子商务网站，表示价格下降

 <h4>Price <s>£15</s> <span>£9</span> </h4>


人们通常更喜欢使用 span 标签，然后在 CSS 中添加一个 text-decoration 属性，但是这非常方便

它还可以用于注释其他类型的文本。它可以防止我们编写沉闷的 CSS

 <ruby>帶翅膀<rt>Carmine</rt></ruby> <ruby>的老鼠<rt>Falcone</rt></ruby>

该标签通常用于创建用户可以打开和关闭的交互式小部件。 默认情况下，小部件是关闭的。 打开时，它会展开并显示其中的内容。

 <details> <summary>Click me to see the Answer</summary> <p> The Ability to speak doesn't make you intelligent .</p> </details>

这实际上非常有用，它在我们的 Select 标签中添加了额外的样式，使其更具吸引力和组织性如果您有很长的选项列表，则相关选项组更容易为用户处理。

 <form > <label for="swords">Choose a Sword:</label> <select name="swords" id="swords"> <optgroup label="Valyrian Steel"> <option value="Oathkeeper">Oathkeeper</option> <option value="Widows Wail">Widows Wail</option> </optgroup> <optgroup label="Knifes"> <option value="mercedes">Needle</option> </optgroup> </select> <br><br> <input type="submit" value="Submit"> </form>

这也称为量规。 它也非常实用，因为我们可以定义仪表颜色应该改变的百分比/值，例如，如果您给出的值小于 33（即低值），仪表的颜色将类似地变为红色以达到高和最佳, 这是一个非常有用的 HTML 标签，就好像你倾向于做 CSS 一样，我们最终可能会写出很长的 CSS

示例：磁盘使用情况、查询结果的相关性等。

 <p> 0L <meter id="fuel" min="0" max="100" low="33" high="66" optimum="80" value="20"> at 50/100 </meter> 5L</p> <p> 0L <meter id="fuel" min="0" max="100" low="33" high="66" optimum="80" value="50"> at 50/100 </meter> 5L</p> <p> 0L <meter id="fuel" min="0" max="100" low="33" high="66" optimum="80" value="80"> at 50/100 </meter> 5L</p>

通常我看到很多人编写 CSS 来创建进度条，有些人甚至在 react 中创建了单独的组件

<label for="file">File progress:</label> <progress id="file" max="100" value="70"> 70% </progress>

里面的内容以浏览器默认的等宽字体显示。 当我发现这并没有被弃用时，我感到很惊讶，因为大多数人最终选择设置跨度样式

<p>Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>C</kbd> to copy text (Windows).</p>


它可用于创建游戏或动画。这是罕见的 HTML 标签之一，可能是最少使用的

<p><bdo dir="rtl">May the force be with you .</bdo></p>

感谢您阅读本文祝您有美好的一天

斯蒂芬格里菲斯 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Sam-Sundar

Posted on May 14

The HTML tags that are considered important are the ones that are commonly used however that is not the case there are some important HTML tags that most people don't use and end up wasting time on writing equivalent CSS


Let's Get into it

1.The <s> tag
The <s> tag specifies text that is no longer correct, accurate or relevant. The text will be displayed With a line through it The S stands for a Strike-Through Usually used in E-Commerce Website to denote a price drop

<h4>Price
    <s>£15</s>
    <span>£9</span>
</h4>


People often prefer using span tag and then add a text-decoration property in CSS,However this is quite handy

2.The <ruby>, <rt>, and <rp> tags
The <ruby> HTML element represents small annotations that are rendered above, below, or next to base text, usually used for showing the pronunciation of East Asian characters. It can also be used for annotating other kinds of text.It Prevents us from writing dreary CSS

 <ruby>帶翅膀<rt>Carmine</rt></ruby>
 <ruby>的老鼠<rt>Falcone</rt></ruby>

3.The <details>tag
The <details> tag specifies additional details that the user can open and close on demand. The tag is often used to create an interactive widget that the user can open and close. By default, the widget is closed. When open, it expands, and displays the content within.

<details>

  <summary>Click me to see the Answer</summary>
  <p>
      The Ability to speak doesn’t make you intelligent
.</p>
</details>

4.The <optgroup> tag
The <optgroup> tag is used to group related options in a <select> element (drop-down list). This is one is actually quite useful,It adds additional style in our Select tag and makes it a little more attractive and organized If you have a long list of options, groups of related options are easier to handle for a user.

<form >
  <label for="swords">Choose a Sword:</label>
  <select name="swords" id="swords">
    <optgroup label="Valyrian Steel">
      <option value="Oathkeeper">Oathkeeper</option>
      <option value="Widows Wail">Widows Wail</option>
    </optgroup>
    <optgroup label="Knifes">
      <option value="mercedes">Needle</option>

    </optgroup>
  </select>
  <br><br>
  <input type="submit" value="Submit">
</form>

5.The <meter> tag
The <meter> tag defines a scalar measurement within a known range, or a fractional value. This is also known as a gauge. It is also quite functional as we can define at what percent/value the color of meter should change for example if you give a value less than the 33(i.e low value) the color of the meter will turn into red similarly for high and optimum, This is a very useful HTML tag as if you tend to do CSS we may end up writing a long sheets of CSS

<meter>tag saves us a lot of time and efforts

Examples: Disk usage, the relevance of a query result, etc. 

   <p> 0L <meter id="fuel"
       min="0" max="100"
       low="33" high="66" optimum="80"
       value="20">
    at 50/100
</meter> 5L</p>

    <p> 0L <meter id="fuel"
       min="0" max="100"
       low="33" high="66" optimum="80"
       value="50">
    at 50/100
</meter> 5L</p>
    <p> 0L <meter id="fuel"
       min="0" max="100"
       low="33" high="66" optimum="80"
       value="80">
    at 50/100
</meter> 5L</p>

6.The <progress> tag
The <progress> HTML element displays an indicator showing the completion progress of a task, typically displayed as a progress bar. Usually i have seen a lot of people write CSS in order to create a progress bar some have even created separate components in react

<label for="file">File progress:</label>

<progress id="file" max="100" value="70"> 70% </progress>

7.The kbd tag
The <kbd> tag is used to define keyboard input. The content inside is displayed in the browser's default monospace font. I was surprised when i found out that this wasn't deprecated since most people end up opting to style a span instead

<p>Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>C</kbd> to copy text (Windows).</p>


8.The bdo tag BDO stands for Bi-Directional Override.

The <bdo> tag is used to override the current text direction.

It can be used to create games or in animations.This is one of the rare HTML tags and probably the least used 
<p><bdo dir="rtl">May the force be with you
.</bdo></p>

Thank You for reading this Article Have a Good Day

Stephen Griffiths - May 12
item.author: samsundar

item.title: JS：“CTR + Wheel”事件 | JS: "CTR + Wheel" Event
item.description:
这篇简短的阅读适用于那些正在搜索如何获取CTR + MOUSEWHEEL事件的人。

这是一个示例。 假设如果我们CTR + WHEEL ，我们正在尝试更改文本的字体大小。

 /** You can change the window to element you want this event to happen. example: document.getElementById("view-chapter-verse").addEventListener() */ window . addEventListener ( " wheel " , function ( event ) { event . preventDefault (); // this will prevent the scaling for browsers. if ( event . ctrlKey ) { // check if we are pressing the CTR key if ( event . deltaY ) { // Do what you want here. In this example // Im trying to change the Font Size of my article // event.deltaY is less than 0 it means we are scrolling up. If not scrolling down. fontSize = event . deltaY < 0 ? fontSize . value + 0.5 : fontSize . value - 0.5 ; } } });

雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— May 9 5月9日



原文:

This short read is for those who are searching how to get event for CTR + MOUSEWHEEL event.

Here is a sample. Lets say we are trying to change to font size of our text if we CTR + WHEEL.

 /**
You can change the window to element you want this event to happen.
example: document.getElementById("view-chapter-verse").addEventListener()
*/
window.addEventListener("wheel", function (event) { event.preventDefault(); // this will prevent the scaling for browsers. if (event.ctrlKey) { // check if we are pressing the CTR key if (event.deltaY) { // Do what you want here. In this example // Im trying to change the Font Size of my article // event.deltaY is less than 0 it means we are scrolling up. If not scrolling down. fontSize = event.deltaY < 0 ? fontSize.value + 0.5 : fontSize.value - 0.5; } }
});

Jakub Andrzejewski - May 9

item.title: 如何立即调用函数 | How to call a function immediately
item.description:
龙腾cjw

发表于我用下面的代码在我的父组件中写我的请求：

 useEffect(() => { // my request }, [])

众所周知，父组件的useEffect会在子组件之后执行，所以这个请求会在所有子组件渲染后执行。
如何在子组件的 useEffect 之前立即调用我的请求？



原文:

dragooncjw

Posted on May 14

useEffect(() => {
  // my request
}, [])

As we all know, useEffect of parent component will implement after child component, so this request will be implemented after all child components rendered.
How can I call my request immediately before my child component's useEffect?

item.title: Javascript Ajax 的简单练习 | Simple exercise for Javascript Ajax
item.description:
哈米德·哈格杜斯特

发表于我有大约 4 年的编程私人导师工作经验。 大约五年前，我在我的网站上用波斯语发表了一篇题为“ Private Laravel Tutor ”的博文，人们开始打电话给我。 我一直喜欢教人，这是一个很好的机会。 一段时间后，学生人数增加，今天我平均每天有两次课程，我以前的大多数学生都在世界各地工作。 今天我有一个关于使用 AJAX 的 JS 会议。 我在这个社区为初学者朋友发布代码的内容。 

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Learning Ajax</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/app.css"> </head> <body> <button class="btn" id="load">Load Posts</button> <button class="btn" id="clear">Clear Posts</button> <div id="posts"> </div> <script src="js/app.js"></script> </body> </html>
 body { margin: 0; } #posts { background-color: #f5f5f5; display: flex; justify-content: center; flex-wrap: wrap; } .post { width: 25%; margin: 20px; border: 3px solid rgb(36, 220, 91); padding: 10px; } .post:hover { border: 3px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); } .post h1 { font-family: cursive; } .btn { padding: 10px; margin: 10px; background-color: rgb(36, 220, 91); color: black; }
 document.getElementById("load").addEventListener("click", function () { url = "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts"; sendAjax(url); }); function sendAjax(url) { let http = new XMLHttpRequest(); http.onload = function () { let posts_element = document.getElementById("posts"); let posts = JSON.parse(this.responseText); posts.forEach(function (post) { let div = document.createElement("div"); div.className = "post"; let h3 = document.createElement("h3"); h3.textContent = post.title; div.appendChild(h3); let p = document.createElement("p"); p.textContent = post.body; div.appendChild(p); posts_element.appendChild(div); }); }; http.open("GET", url); http.send(); } document.getElementById("clear").addEventListener("click", function () { let posts_element = document.getElementById("posts"); posts_element.textContent = '' });

如果您正在寻找导师，请随时与我联系 :)



原文:

Hamid Haghdoost

Posted on May 14

I have about 4 years of experience working as private tutor of programming. About five years ago I published a blog post in my website in Persian language about entitled "Private Laravel Tutor" and people started to call me. I always love teaching people and it was a good opportunity. After a while the number of student raise up and today I have two sessions per day in average and most of my previous students are working around the world. Today I had a JS session about Working with AJAX. I post the content of the codes for beginner friends at this community.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Learning Ajax</title>

    <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/app.css">
</head>
<body>
    <button class="btn" id="load">Load Posts</button>
    <button class="btn" id="clear">Clear Posts</button>
    <div id="posts">

    </div>
    <script src="js/app.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

body {
    margin: 0;
}

#posts {
    background-color: #f5f5f5;
    display: flex;
    justify-content: center;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
}


.post {
    width: 25%;
    margin: 20px;
    border: 3px solid rgb(36, 220, 91);
    padding: 10px;
}

.post:hover {
    border: 3px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);
}

.post h1 {
    font-family: cursive;
}

.btn {
    padding: 10px;
    margin: 10px;
    background-color: rgb(36, 220, 91);
    color: black;
}


document.getElementById("load").addEventListener("click", function () {
    url = "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts";
    sendAjax(url);
});

function sendAjax(url) {
    let http = new XMLHttpRequest();
    http.onload = function () {
        let posts_element = document.getElementById("posts");
        let posts = JSON.parse(this.responseText);

        posts.forEach(function (post) {
            let div = document.createElement("div");
            div.className = "post";

            let h3 = document.createElement("h3");
            h3.textContent = post.title;
            div.appendChild(h3);

            let p = document.createElement("p");
            p.textContent = post.body;
            div.appendChild(p);

            posts_element.appendChild(div);
        });
    };
    http.open("GET", url);
    http.send();
}

document.getElementById("clear").addEventListener("click", function () {
    let posts_element = document.getElementById("posts");
    posts_element.textContent = ''
});

If you are looking for a tutor, feel free to contact me :)
item.author: haamid

item.title: 十个键盘快捷键来提升你的终端游戏 | Ten Keyboard Shortcuts To Up Your Terminal Game
item.description:
如果您不熟悉终端，它可能会令人生畏。 键入命令不一定带来与在文字处理软件中编辑文本相同的便利。 使用箭头键清除错误输入的单词或使用退格键清除错误输入的命令会很快变得非常乏味。 如果您正在寻找一种使用键盘快捷键让您的终端体验更愉快的方法，那么您刚刚找到了正确的文章。

本文将介绍我的 10 个常用的终端键盘快捷键。 我在 macOS 系统上使用Zsh与Oh My Zsh框架相结合的终端。 但是，这些键盘快捷键也适用于Bash 。

不知道确切的目录名称或不想输入那个长文件或目录名称？ 只需输入前几个字符，然后点击选项卡按钮即可激活自动完成功能！

无需按箭头键几次来移动每个字符的光标。 在节省一些击键的同时将整个单词向左移动不是很好吗？

当然，在相反的方向上也应该可以通过整个单词向前推进。

如果在输入整个命令后，您得出的结论是您忘记在行首添加特定单词怎么办？ 那应该完全没有问题，只需一个简单的按键将光标移动到行首！

您也可能需要移动到命令的末尾以添加一些额外的参数。 这也是可能的，没有任何麻烦。

无需多次按退格键即可删除单词。 使用此键盘快捷键可以节省一些时间。

您是否将错误的命令粘贴到终端中？ 只需使用简单的键盘快捷键即可删除整行！

您之前的某个命令是否有一堆文本输出？ 您想清除整个终端屏幕以便从头开始吗？ 您可以在终端中键入 clear 命令以获得最终结果。 那是 4 次击键。 谁有时间做这个？ 使用此键盘快捷键，我们可以更快地完成它！

您是否为终端模拟器打开了一堆选项卡或窗口？ 您可以使用此键盘快捷键一一关闭它们。

您是否刚刚在终端中使用命令启动了应用程序？ 有一种非常简单的方法可以关闭此应用程序，这可能是最方便的键盘快捷键之一，可用于处理无响应应用程序的许多场景。

如果内容有帮助，请随时在这里支持我：



原文:

The terminal can be intimidating if you're not familiar with it. Typing in commands doesn't necessarily bring the same convenience as editing text in a word-processing software. Using your arrow keys to clear a mistyped word or backspacing to clear up your mistyped command can become very tedious very quickly. If you are looking for a way to make your terminal experience more enjoyable with keyboard shortcuts, you have just found the right article. 

This article will cover my 10 commonly used keyboard shortcuts for the terminal. I make use of the terminal on a macOS system with Zsh combined with the Oh My Zsh framework. However, these keyboard shortcuts will also work with Bash.

1. TAB


Don't know the exact directory name or are not in the mood to type that long file or directory name? Just type the first few characters and then hit the tab button to activate autocomplete!

2. Option + Left Arrow


No need to press your arrow keys a couple of times to move the cursor per character. Wouldn't it be nice to move whole words to the left while saving some keystrokes?

3. Option + Right Arrow


Moving forward by whole words should of course also be possible in the opposite direction. 

4. CTRL + A


What if after you've entered your whole command, you come to the conclusion that you forgot to add a particular word at the beginning of a line? That should be no problem at all, just a simple keystroke to move the cursor to the beginning of the line!

5. CTRL + E


It's also possible that you need to move to the end of the command to add something extra parameters. That's also possible without any trouble.

6. CTRL + W


No need to hit the backspace a couple of times to delete a word. Save yourself some time with this keyboard shortcut. 

7. CTRL + U


Did you paste the wrong command into the terminal? Just delete the entire line with a simple keyboard shortcut!

8. CTRL + L


Do you have a bunch of text output from one of your previous commands? Would you like to clear the whole terminal screen so you can start with a clean slate? You can type the clear command in your terminal to get the end result. That's 4 keystrokes. Who's got time for that? We can do it even more quickly with this keyboard shortcut! 

9. CTRL + D


Do you have a bunch of tabs or windows open for your Terminal emulator? You can close them one by one by using this keyboard shortcut.

10. CTRL + C


Did you just start up an application with a command in the terminal? There is a very simple way to close this application and this may be one of the handiest keyboard shortcuts that can be used in many scenarios in which you are dealing with an unresponsive application.

If the content was helpful, feel free to support me here:
item.author: duncanlew

item.title: Por que devemos ter cuidado com EXEMPLOS DE SUCESSO | Por que devemos ter cuidado com EXEMPLOS DE SUCESSO
item.description:
奥拉！ 梅莫。 哦？

呸！ 有可能吗？ 例如，existe 1 chance em 1 milhão de uma pessoa ser atingida por um raio。 标志，é pouco provável ser atingido por um raio。 圣科萨斯不同。 我faltam dados。 呸！ Eu não vou dizer o que você vai decidir fazer pra sua vida. Eu não vou dizer o que você vai decidir fazer pra sua vida。

Eu vou sugerir a pergunta que você precisa tentar responder ao ler histórias de sucesso: Eu vou sugerir a pergunta que você precisatentar responder ao ler histórias de sucesso:

É possível, mas quais as chances ? Épossível, mas quais 作为机会？



原文:


Olá! Esse post é pra você que é iniciante em programação, está em transição de carreira ou mesmo pra qualquer pessoa. Mesmo. Vivemos uma época de venda de ilusões e esse post é para sacudir você de um possível transe provocado por esse mercado. Vem comigo.

Hoje em dia não é incomum encontrarmos posts na internet, em grupo, vídeos em tiktok (além das dancinhas), youtube, gente na twitch, o que for, vendendo ilusões, especialmente nas áreas de empreendedorismo e programação. Tem inúmeras revistas, inclusive, que falam sobre salários de programadores serem de 20k, empreendedores dizendo que o salário de dev chega a 5k em 6 meses, uma loucura. E é fácil sermos levados a acreditar nessas coisas, especialmente quando estamos começando numa carreira, pois é o que queremos escutar.

Muita gente fala essas coisas pra vender jornal, pra ganhar views, mesmo sabendo que não é real. Ou é?

Eu tenho um primo que ganha 9k dolares e é tech lead e não fez faculdade, logo, faculdade é inútil

Eu tenho um amigo que ganha 5k e começou na área há 4 meses, trabalha pra uma empresa americana

Eu tenho um sobrinho que fez um app e vendeu pra google e ficou podre de rico

Eu tenho uma conhecida que saiu do país sem faculdade, trabalha na alemanha e ganha em euro!

Muito provavelmente você já ouviu alguma dessas histórias de alguém... ou da própria pessoa, quando defendendo um ponto contra cursar uma faculdade ou insinuando que é fácil ganhar dinheiro na área com pouco esforço.

Essas histórias são mentirosas, são fake news? Não! Realmente, existem pessoas que venderam apps pra grandes empresas e ficaram ricas, existem pessoas que conseguiram auferir excelentes salários sem faculdade, em pouco tempo e certamente existem pessoas que saíram do Brasil para trabalhar fora sem ter concluído a universidade, eu tenho amigos e amigas inclusive que estão aqui em Portugal comigo, ganhando bem e sem faculdade.

Existe outro grupo de pessoas que propaga essas histórias e acabam praticando a venda de ilusão, até com boas intenções, porque conhecem esses exemplos. Às vezes elas são esses exemplos. Elas acreditam que é possível porque elas fizeram ou conhecem pessoas que fizeram. E, certamente, isso prova que é possível.

A diferença é que ser possível NÃO SIGNIFICA ser provável.

E essa diferença confunde muita gente: quem propaga e quem consome a informação. E isso se transforma em ilusão.

O que é possível? Possível é algo que pode acontecer, não tem uma contrapartida estatística... por exemplo, é possível que você seja atingido por um raio, que você ganhe na loteria... quando falamos que algo é possível, estamos falando em 0 ou 1. Logo, é possível ser atingido por um raio.

O que é provável? Provável é um termo usado para lidarmos com a chance de algo acontecer, tem uma contrapartida estatística, porque para definir se algo é provável ou não, é preciso dados. Por exemplo, existe 1 chance em 1 milhão de uma pessoa ser atingida por um raio. Logo, é pouco provável ser atingido por um raio. E probabilidade é uma faixa de valores, não 0 ou 1.

Ou seja, tudo que é provável precisa ser possível. Mas nem tudo que é possível, precisa ser provável. São coisas diferentes. Trazendo pro nosso mundo da programação:

  • é possível ganhar 5k em 6 meses, mas quais as chances?
  • é possível sair do país sem faculdade com programação, mas quais as chances?
  • é possível ganhar um grande salário e atingir posições de liderança, mas quais as chances?

Repare que eu não estou te dando a resposta. Me faltam dados. O meu intuito aqui não é "advogar pro outro lado". Não é dizer: "façam faculdade", "façam concurso público", "façam isso ou aquilo". Não! Eu não vou dizer o que você vai decidir fazer pra sua vida.

Eu vou sugerir a pergunta que você precisa tentar responder ao ler histórias de sucesso:

É possível, mas quais as chances?
item.author: rlage

item.title: 创业的故事 | The Story of a Startup
item.description:
安东·古巴列夫

发表于在这篇文章中，我将讲述我是如何尝试创建和启动一家初创公司的，当时我只有一点空闲时间和一个特定的目标。 我敢肯定，许多开发人员都想在自己的项目上工作，并且不止一次地考虑过。 我希望对我的错误的描述能帮助其他人不要这样做。

主意

所以，那是在 2016 年左右。我正在从事定制开发和支持，也就是外包。 我有自己的小团队（3-5 名开发人员）、办公室和常客。 这家小企业很成功，带来了收入，养活了我的家人，并为项目带来了乐趣。 但是，我希望在产品选择、技术解决方案和财务独立方面有更多的自由。 自我实现的愿望也让我晚上无法入睡。 工程师的灵魂需要自己的产品，以及对我来说似乎正确和有趣的战略和路线图。 当然，产量比当时高一点。 我的项目似乎可以提供这一切并让我感到高兴，因为招募的关键数量的付费客户可以不断提供所需的盈利水平，而外包客户可能会在最不合时宜的时刻离开，而且他的需求并不总是一致的与我的愿望。

该项目的想法并非凭空产生。 我在工作中遇到了一个活生生的问题。 这是财务会计。 由于客户为工作时间付费，因此每个月有数百小时要计算，要出具报告，要与客户核对账目，要确定收入到支出，要确定损失地点。 税务报告和工资也很耗时。 由于公司规模很小，不可能聘请会计师。 当时不存在合适的自动化系统。 一个灵活且对我国税法敏感的系统。
我的金融工作从简单开始，逐渐演变并变得更加复杂。 下表显示了我如何尝试计划收入和支出、计算一小时的最低盈利成本以及计算税款。

有一段时间就够了，但有一点我意识到我没有看到重要的细节。 例如，团队中每个人的效率如何，他为自己的工作付出了多少或带来了损失，软件、快递等成本的合理性如何。我需要详细信息。 渐渐地，桌子变成了这样：

当然，数字都是编出来的。 这只是我使用的表的一小部分，不幸的是其余的都没有幸存下来。 在某些时候，我开始怀念表格的功能。 更确切地说，他们的开发成本已经变得非常高。 我开始寻找一些更专业和基于盒子的解决方案，可以立即解决我的大部分问题。 我们并没有真正在表格中做 CRM）虽然有可能，但还是有解决方案的。
所以我所有的搜索都以失败告终。 那些可以找到的解决方案是针对与财务不同的会计。 它提供的计划和分析很少。 在这些程序中记录了事实和税收计算，这当然也是必要和有用的，但根本不是缺少什么并且不能解决问题。

第一次尝试

从那一刻起，我决定这就是主意！ 我是一名开发人员，我可以编写代码！ 最终产品有大量的热情、想法和模糊的愿景。 但我真的很想尽快拿到第一个原型。 虽然当时我并没有考虑原型，但我正在考虑一个具有很多可能性的成品。 在我第一次尝试时，我使用了我每天使用的工具。 那个时候是一个框架yii2在前端我什么都没用，除了 jQuery 就够了。 我开始工作了。 我立即从熟悉的设计师那里订购了徽标和登录页面。 熟悉的制造商迅速将布局登陆页面转换为 HTML。 这个名字立即出现）当然为它买了一个域名。

不幸的是，登陆页面以及第一版的源代码都没有幸存下来，但源代码中仍然没有任何有趣的东西。
不到一个月，我已经开始失去热情了。 随着时间的推移，我在空闲时间工作，结果非常小。 一个或多或少的工作部分与我想象的多功能产品完全不同。 当他开始解决一个问题时，他意识到它被另外两个问题挡住了。地平线上出现了一个在发射前几个月的漫长工作。 所有这一切都非常令人沮丧，逐渐放弃了第一次尝试。 我对工作毫无准备，低估了他们。

第二次尝试

然而，对我的项目的渴望仍然没有放弃。 它每天都来找我，最后六个月我决定再试一次。 我试图考虑过去的错误并从原型开始。 在 Axure 中，我绘制了最终结果，甚至效果很好。 幸运的是，从第一次尝试也有了发展：源代码库、登陆页面、体验。 我不必从头开始，所以我又开始编码了。 这一次，多亏了原型，事情变得更好了。 我更了解我在做什么，以及为什么我不必重做工作代码，因为从 UI/UX 的角度来看，结果看起来不像它应该的那样。

在这种模式下，已经过去了大约 3 个月。 我在空闲时间继续工作。 这里发生了一个非常有趣和必要的提议。 那时我已经准备好将工作形式从外包改为公司。 收入被冻结并停止增长。 当我从一名工程师进一步发展成为一名经理和一名企业家时，我似乎已经达到了天花板。 而且我根本不想要它，因为对我来说编码曾经并且仍然更有趣。 所以我接受了这个提议，我的生活发生了翻天覆地的变化。 收入增加了，但空闲时间不再存在。 以及能量。 这是一个重新格式化以适应新生活方式的时期。 当然，在这种情况下，该项目再次被放弃。 这一次，我还没有为变化的环境做好准备。

一年之后

我在一家新公司工作，我很高兴也很感兴趣。 但有一天我碰巧发现有一家初创公司跟我的想法一模一样。 这就像一道闪电。

很抱歉我找不到英文的截图。 这是一个包含收入和支出列表的部分，以及一个图表和现金中断的预测。 是的，这一切都与多功能联合收割机相去甚远。 该项目的作者推出了 MVP，并立即付款并立即获得了销售。 作者并没有开发一堆功能，而是制作了一个即使在最小模式下也可以使用的产品。 我当然不高兴。 我的想法是活的并且需要，但我无法实现它，而其他人接受了它。

几乎立刻，我发现了第二个这样的项目。 它与第一个有点不同，它有点糟糕，它更好，它更好，但它有效并且它的创始人有利可图。

这是最后一根稻草，我决定进行第三次尝试。 我不能放弃一个最终如此受欢迎的想法。

第三次尝试

在过去的尝试中，每次都存在严重的规划问题。 这主要是关于计划我的时间以及首先将其投资在哪里。 当我可以做这个项目时，我为自己找到了一个时间，这样就有机会关注家庭和孩子。

  • 周一至周日 21 至 0 小时。 孩子上床睡觉的时间。
  • 都是全职。 决定捐出一个假期。
  • 下午 2-3 小时，孩子们睡着了。 在这一切中，你需要休息的时间我扣除了10%，这样的时间肯定会。 现在我有一张我可以支配的时间资源的图片。 这已经可以更客观地评估我可以做什么以及在什么时间范围内。 鉴于前两次放弃的尝试，我不排除第三次可能出错。 而且我不想浪费很多时间。 所以我需要B计划。我准备好了。 首先，在任何情况下都必须使用项目的源代码。 例如，开放源代码并作为示例代码或投资组合展示给潜在雇主。 因此，必须尽可能负责任地处理他的写作质量。 我必须做的第二件事是选择可以用来帮助我找到最佳工作的技术。 当时的趋势是 Symfony 和 Angular，我选择了它们。 与他们的经验根本没有。 从那时起，这个备份对我帮助很大，但稍后再说。 使用 Symfony 一个月后，我并没有经常打开文档，因为我负责这个框架并且只是编写代码。 但是对于 Angular，一切都变得更加复杂。 由于前端不是我真正的，而且我有一点经验，所以我决定使用 VueJs，它更容易使用。 已经六个月了。 我已经有一个或多或少的工作原型和一个我自己构建并花了很多时间的好的设计。 但是，部队的存量枯竭了。 在竞争对手中出现了越来越多的新功能，为了跟上它们，我显然不能，而​​且发布并不是一切。 我试着寻找合作伙伴。 一个会承担一些工作的人，比如市场营销或用户体验之类的，所以我可以专注于开发。 但事实证明，没有像我这样的爱好者愿意每周免费工作 60 到 70 小时。 我试图吸引投资者。 但这不适合我。 首先，我对适度的注入和艰苦的工作条件对非常大的股票不感兴趣。 其次，投资者希望我做很多管理工作而不是开发，这也不符合我所做的事情的目的。 第三，它始终是一份全职工作。 我负担不起这样的冒险，因为那时我已经有了一个家庭。 第三次尝试在开始后大约 7-8 个月后被淹死。 根据所花费时间的报告，您可以看到热情是如何燃烧和消退的）在仅第三次尝试的数量中，我花费了超过 500 小时的空闲时间。 几年后，我意识到，即使是免费的，也有可能并且有必要布置最低限度的功能。 第一批用户肯定会激发热情，也许现在情况会有所不同。

结论

在这个故事中，重要的不是这个项目最终有多成功。 最重要的是获得的经验和我能够从这一切中获得的好处。
一方面，启动似乎失败了。 是的，但这就是我得到的有价值的东西。

  • 获得了使用 Symfony 的经验，这最终帮助我满足了这个框架的要求，并让我的薪水几乎增加了两倍。 我可以说我确实实现了一个目标
  • 从业务负责人、产品负责人甚至项目经理的角度来看发展。 它让我现在能够更好地了解这些人，并且更经常地找到共同点并找到真正合适的解决方案
  • 我克服了创业的想法，了解了所有的利弊，现在它几乎不打扰我了）我变得更加欣赏雇主并尊重他们的工作。
  • 我学会了计划我的工作，这帮助我成功地领导了我的项目一段时间，这也是我职业生涯中另一个有用的里程碑。
  • 最重要的是，与家人共度的时间变得更加宝贵。

我很高兴我的生活中有过这样的经历，但我不能向其他人推荐它来重复它。 让每个人自己决定，希望这能帮助他做出正确的决定。

项目来源：

后端
前端

如果有人可以打破规则并启动他们的项目，我会很高兴

Tek Bahadur Kshetri - Tek Bahadur Kshetri - May 13 5月13日



原文:

Anton Gubarev

Posted on May 14

In this article I will tell the story of how I tried to create and launch a startup, having only a little free time and a specific goal. I’m sure many developers would like to work on own project and have thought about it more than once. I hope that the description of my mistakes will help others not to do the same.

Idea

So, it was around 2016. I was working on custom development and support, that is, outsourcing. I had my own small team (3-5 developers), office and regular customers. This small business was successful, bringing income, feeding my family, and giving pleasure to projects. However, I wanted more freedom in product selection, technical solutions, financial independence. The desire to self-realization also did not allow me to sleep at night. The soul of an engineer demanded its own product, with the strategy and the roadmap as it seems right and interesting to me. And of course, the yield is a little higher than it was at the time. And it seemed that my project could give all this and make me happy, because the recruited critical mass of paid customers can provide the desired level of profitability constantly, while the outsourcer client may leave at the most inopportune moment and not always his needs will coincide with my wishes.

The idea of the project was not born out of nowhere. I had a living and constant problem in the work. This is financial accounting. Since customers paid for the working hours, there were hundreds of hours to be counted each month, reports to be issued, accounts to be reconciled with customers, income to expenses, loss locations to be identified. Tax reports and salaries were also time-consuming. Since the business was very small, it was not possible to maintain an accountant. A suitable automated system did not exist at that time. A system that will be flexible and sensitive to my country’s tax laws.
My work with finance started out simple and gradually evolved and became more complex. The table below shows how I tried to plan my income and expenses, calculate the minimum profitable cost of an hour and calculate taxes.

It was enough for a while, but there came a point when I realized I didn’t see the important details. For example, how effective each person in the team, he pays for his work or brings a loss, how justified the cost of software, courier, etc. I needed details. And gradually the table was like this:

Of course, the figures are all made up. And this is only a small part of the tables that I used, the rest unfortunately did not survive. At some point I started to miss the features of the tables. Rather, to say that the cost of their development has become very high. I started looking for some more specialized and box-based solutions that would solve most of my problems immediately. We don’t really do CRMs in tables) Although it’s possible, there are solutions.
So all my searches ended in failure. Those solutions that could be found were directed to accounting, which is different from financial. It provides very little planning and analysis. In these programs there was a recording of facts and tax calculation, it is of course also necessary and useful, but not at all what is missing and does not solve the problem.

First try

From that moment I decided that this is the idea! I am a developer and I can code it! There was a huge amount of enthusiasm, ideas and hazy vision of the final product. But I really wanted to get the first prototype as soon as possible. Although at the time I wasn’t thinking about the prototype, I was thinking about a finished product with lots of possibilities. On my first try, I took the tools I used every day. At that time it was a framework yii2 On the frontend I did not use anything except jQuery and this was enough. VueJS was released later.
I started work. I immediately ordered a logo and landing page from a familiar designer. The familiar maker quickly turned the layout landing page into HTML. And the name came up immediately) Of course bought a domain for it.

Landing page unfortunately did not survive, as well as the source code of the first version, but there is still nothing in the source code that could be of interest.
It’s been less than a month, and I’ve already started to lose enthusiasm. As time went on, I worked in my free time, and the result was very small. One more or less working section that was nothing like the multi-functional product I had imagined. As he began to solve one problem, he realized that it was blocked by two more etc. On the horizon appeared a long work for many months before the launch. All this was very demotivated and gradually the first attempt was abandoned. I was unprepared for the work and underestimated them.

Second try

However, the desire for my project still did not let go. It came to me every day and in the end six months I decided to try again. I tried to take into account the mistakes of the past and started with the prototype. In Axure I drew the end result, even worked out well. Fortunately, there were also developments from the first attempt: source code base, landing page, experience. I didn’t have to start from scratch, so I started coding again. This time, things were going much better thanks to the prototypes. I understood more what I was doing and why I didn’t have to redo the working code because the result doesn’t look like it should from the point of view of UI/UX.

In this mode, about 3 months have passed. I continued to work in my free time. And here happens an offer, very interesting and necessary. At that time I was ready to change the format of work from outsourcing to the company. Revenues were frozen in place and stopped growing. It seems that I have reached the ceiling when to further develop from an engineer it was necessary to become a manager and an entrepreneur. And I didn’t want it at all, because coding for me was and remains more interesting. So I accepted this offer and my life changed dramatically. The income grew, but the free time ceased to be. As well as the energy. It was a period of reformatting to a new lifestyle. And of course the project was abandoned again in such conditions. This time I was not ready for the changed circumstances.

One year later

I was working at a new company, and I was happy and interested. But one day I happen to find out that there’s a startup that’s exactly like my idea. It was like a lightning strike.

I’m sorry I can’t find a screenshot in English. Here’s a section with a list of income and expenses, and a graph and a prediction of cash breaks. Yes, it was all far from the multi-functional combine. The author of the project launched MVP and immediately it was paid and immediately he had sales. The author did not develop a bunch of features and instead made a product that could be used even now in the minimum mode. Of course I was upset. My idea was alive and needed, and I couldn’t realize it and someone else took it.

And almost immediately, I discover a second project like this. It’s a little bit different than the first, it’s a little worse, it’s better, it’s better, but it works and it’s profitable for its founders.

It was the last straw and I decided to make a third attempt. I could not just throw away an idea that ended up being so popular.

Third try

In past attempts, every time there were serious problems in planning. It was primarily about planning the time I had and where to invest it first. I found a time for myself when I can do the project, so that there is an opportunity to pay attention to family and children too.

  • Mon through Sun from 21 to 0 hours. Time when children go to bed.
  • All full-time. It was decided to donate one holiday.
  • 2-3 hours in the afternoon when the children were asleep. Of all this, I deducted 10% for periods when you need to rest, and such periods will definitely be. Now I have a picture of what time resource I have at my disposal. This could already allow for a more objective assessment of what I can do and in what time frame. Given the first two abandoned attempts I did not rule out that the third could go wrong. And I would not like to waste a lot of time. So I needed plan B. And I prepared it. First, the source code of the project must be used in any case. For example, put on open source and show as an example code or portfolio to prospective employers. Therefore, the quality of his writing had to be approached as responsibly as possible. The second thing I had to do was choose the technology that I could use to help me find the best jobs. At that point in the trend were Symfony and Angular and it was them I chose. Experience with them had no at all. This backup has helped me a lot since then, but about that later. After a month of Symfony, I didn’t open docs often, because I took care of this framework and just coded. But with Angular everything was more complicated. Since the frontend is not really mine, and the experience I had a little, I decided to take VueJs, which was much easier to work with. It’s been six months. I already have a more or less working prototype and a good design that I built myself and spent a lot of time on it. But, the stock of forces dried up. At competitors appeared more and more new features and to keep up with them I obviously could not and the launch was not everything. I tried looking for partners. A person who will take on some of the work, like marketing or UX or something, so I can focus on the development. But it turned out that there were no enthusiasts like me who were willing to work 60 to 70 hours a week for free. I tried to appeal to investors. But this did not suit me. First, I was not interested in very large shares for a modest infusion and hard working conditions. Second, investors wanted me to do a lot of management work instead of development, which also didn’t fit my purpose for what I was doing. Third, it is always a full time job. I could not afford such an adventure, as I already had a family at that time. The third attempt was drowned after about 7-8 months from the start. According to the report on the time spent, you can see how the enthusiasm burns and fades) In the amount of only a third attempt I spent more than 500 hours of free time. After a few years, I realized that even then it was possible and necessary to lay out the minimum functionality, even if free. The first users would certainly boost enthusiasm and perhaps now things would be different.

Conclusion

What matters in this story is not how successful the project turned out to be. The most important thing is the experience gained and the benefit I was able to derive from all this.
On the one hand, it seems that the startup failed. Yes, but that’s what I got valuable.

  • Got the experience with Symfony, which eventually helped me get to work with the requirement of this framework and increase my salary almost twice. I could say that I did achieve one goal
  • Looked at the development through the eyes of the business owner, the product owner and even the project manager. It allows me now to better understand these people and more often find common ground and find really suitable solutions
  • I got over the idea of startup, understood all the pros and cons and now it hardly bothers me) I have become more appreciative of employers and respect their work.
  • I learned to plan my work, which helped me to successfully lead my projects for a while, and it was also another useful milestone in my career.
  • And most importantly, the time spent with family has become more valuable.

I am very glad that I have had this experience in my life, but I cannot recommend it to others to repeat it. Let each one decide for himself, and hope this will help him to make the right decision.

Project sources:

Backend
Frontend

I will be happy if someone could bend the rules and launch their project

Tek Bahadur Kshetri - May 13
item.author: antgubarev

item.title: 如何准备在线期末考试以获得 A+ | How To Prepare For Final Exam Online To Score A+
item.description:
你已经到了学期末，这意味着期末考试即将来临。 随着学期接近尾声，最后几周的课程作业可能特别苛刻。 很多学生都在忙于就业、作业、期末项目和期末考试。 对于一些人来说，这可能是他们第一次在网上参加期末考试，因为在 COVID-19 流行之后，UGA 转向在线教学。

以下是在线考试帮助的一些有用提示，以便您在期末考试期间取得优异成绩。

在线完成期末考试的提示

提前准备

尽管这似乎是最明显的学习建议，但拖延症在学习中可能发挥重要作用。 提前做好准备尤为重要。 这是为了避免拖延，因为准备许多考试可能会让人不知所措。

写下任何最终项目的截止日期和测试日期。 或者测试是实现这一目标的一种方法。 这将使提前计划变得容易。 并确定何时展开学习的最佳时间。 与其为期末考试而死记硬背，不如每天在考试前学习一点。

试试 Evernote，而不是在 Word 文档或 Google Doc 中输入所有笔记。 通过在笔记中添加链接、清单、表格、附件和录音，你可以使用 Evernote 将所有内容保存在一个地方。

手写笔记也可以导入，最棒的是它们是可搜索的。 您想与其他学生分享您的笔记吗？ 如果您将他们添加为合作者，您可以与他们进行远距离合作。

维护您的资源

每个班级的信息分开，以便于查找并且不会与其他信息混淆。 按类别对信息进行颜色编码是一个有用的提示。 每个班级都应该有自己的颜色，与松散的纸质文件夹的颜色相匹配。

彩色笔可用于在计划器中标记重要日期。 并在您的计算机上指定文件。 如果你更有条理，你的学习会更有效率。

为自己制作一份学习或期末考试指南

在大学里很少有学习指南。 这就是为什么你应该很好地理解你将在期末考试中评估的章节和主题。

通过使用材料创建自己的学习指南来充分利用这一点。 这些就像您的教科书、课堂笔记和教授的 PowerPoint。 因此，您的所有信息都将集中在一个位置并易于访问。

从您的资源中获得最大收益

在学习方面，有各种应用程序和网站可供选择。 当需要休息时，Timeout 会轻轻地淡出您的屏幕，然后在休息结束时将其恢复。 如果您赶时间，休息时间可以自定义和跳过。

如果您需要休息一下，Spotify、Headspace 和 7 Minute Workout 等应用程序可能会帮助您理清思路。 如果您对内容有疑虑或需要有关如何更有效学习的建议，请向您的讲师和同学寻求帮助。

让我们在这里结束吧！

我希望这 5 个在线期末考试技巧可以帮助您准备考试。 除此之外，您还可以从专业人士那里获得最好的在线考试帮助。 他们将指导您正确、轻松地回答您的问题。 立即访问该网站并查看该服务的更多功能！



原文:

You have made it to the end of the semester, which means the final exam is approaching. As the semester draws to a close, the last weeks of coursework can particularly demanding. So many students are juggling employment, assignments, end-of-semester projects, and final examinations. And for some, this may be their first time taking final exams online since UGA switched to online instruction in the wake of the COVID-19 epidemic.

Here are some helpful hints for exam help online so that you can excel during finals season.

Tip to ace your final exam online

Prepare in advance

Although this may appear to be the most apparent study advice, procrastination can play a significant role in studying. It's especially crucial to prepare ahead. That is to avoid procrastinating since preparing for many examinations might be overwhelming.

Writing down due dates and test dates for any final projects. Or tests are one approach to achieve this. This will make it easy to plan ahead. And determine when the optimal time is to spread out your studies. Instead of cramming for your final exam, attempt to study a little each day leading up to test day.

Evernote is a free app that works on iOS, Android, and the desktop

Try Evernote instead of entering all of your notes in a Word document or a Google Doc. You can save everything in one place with Evernote by adding links, checklists, tables, attachments, and audio recordings to your notes.

Handwritten notes may also import, and the greatest thing is that they're searchable. Do you want to share your notes with another student? You may collaborate with them from afar if you add them as a collaborator.

Maintain your resources

Separate information for each class so that it is easy to find and does not get jumbled up with other information. Color-coding information by class is one useful tip. Each class should have its own color that matches the color of the loose paper folder.

Colored pens may be used to mark significant dates in a planner. And to designate papers on your computer. Your studies will be more efficient if you are more organized.

Make a study or final exam guide for yourself

It is uncommon in college to be given a study guide. That is why you should have a good understanding of the chapters and subjects you'll be evaluated on in your final.

Make the most of this by creating your own study guide using materials. These are like your textbook, class notes, and your professor's PowerPoint. All of your information will be in one location and easily accessible as a result of this.

Get maximum from your resources

When it comes to studying, there are various applications and websites to choose from. Quizlet is an online resource/app that allows you to design your own flashcard collection as well as generate personalized quizzes, tests, and activities to help in your learning.

When it's time for a break, Timeout fades your screen out gently and then brings it back when the break gets over. Breaks can customize and skip if you're in a hurry.

If you need a break from studying, applications like Spotify, Headspace, and 7 Minute Workout might help you clear your mind. Use your lecturers and classmates for help if you have concerns about the content or need suggestions on how to study more effectively.

Let's finish up here!

I Hope these 5 tips for the final exam online will help you to prepare for the exams. Besides this, you take the best exam help online from professionals. They will guide you to answer your questions correctly and easily. Visit the website and check more features of the service now!

item.title: 在 Drupal 8 中设置图像样式 | Setting up image styles in Drupal 8
item.description:
瓦迪姆·菲利莫诺夫

发表于网站上显示的所有图像都必须具有图像样式。 否则，它们将以原始大小显示。

转到“配置”→“图像样式”：

 /admin/config/media/image-styles

单击“添加图像样式”按钮→写入名称并更改机器名称。

现在我们需要添加一个效果。 首先，了解两个就足够了：

  • 缩放；
  • 缩放和裁剪。

缩放将按比例减小图像的大小：

如果您有一张 2000x1000 的图像并将其缩放为 500x500，则结果将是一张 500x250 的图片。

缩放和裁剪会将图像设置为您指定的尺寸：

如果您有一个 2000x1000 的图像并且您缩放并裁剪到 500x500，那么结果将是一个 500x500 的图像。

去控制输出图像的实体的显示：

在设置中，选择我们创建的图像样式→保存。

如果图像未更新，请使用以下命令：

 drush image-flush --all ; drush cr



原文:

Vadim Filimonov

Posted on May 14

All images displayed on the site must have an image style. Otherwise they will be displayed in their original size.

Go to "Configuration" → "Image Styles":

/admin/config/media/image-styles

Click the "Add Image Style" button → Write the name and change the machine name.

Now we need to add an effect. To begin with, it is enough to know about two:

  • Scaling;
  • Scaling and Cropping.

Scaling will proportionally reduce the size of your image:

If you have an image of 2000x1000 and you scale it 500x500, the result will be a picture of 500x250.

Scale and crop will set the image to the dimensions you specify:

If you have an image of 2000x1000 and you scale and crop to 500x500, the result will be an image of 500x500.

Go to control the display of the entity that outputs the image:

In the settings, select the image style we created → Save.

If the images are not updated, use the command:

drush image-flush --all ; drush cr
