item.title: 什么是地图对象及其应用 | What is Map object and its applications
item.description:
让我们了解一下 JavaScript 地图。

地图类似于对象。
它们将元素存储在键/值对中。

但是，与对象不同，映射可以包含对象、函数和其他数据类型作为键。
我们可以使用 new Map() 构造函数创建一个 Map。

要将其插入地图，我们使用 set() 方法。 我们也可以使用对象和函数作为键。

 const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); console . log ( map1 ) //expected output: Map {"a": 1, "b": 2}

要访问元素，我们使用 get() 方法。 我们在key上调用get方法，获取对应的值。

 const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); console . log ( map1 . get ( ' a ' )); // expected output: 1

为了检查地图中是否存在元素，我们得到一个名为 has() 的函数

const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); console . log ( map1 . has ( ' c ' )) //expected output: false

然后我们有 clear() 和 delete() 方法可以让我们从地图中删除数据

const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); map1 . delete ( ' b ' ); console . log ( map1 ) //expected output: Map {"a" : 1}

要获得地图的长度，我们有size属性，它将为我们提供地图上存在的元素数量。

 const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); map1 . set ( ' c ' , 3 ); console . log ( map1 . size ); // expected output: 3

要遍历地图，我们可以使用 for 循环或 for-each 循环。 元素按照它们被插入的顺序被访问。

 const map1 = new Map (); map1 . set ( ' a ' , 1 ); map1 . set ( ' b ' , 2 ); map1 . set ( ' c ' , 3 ); for ( let [ key , value ] of map1 ){ console . log ( key , " - " , value ) } // a - 1​ // b - 2​ // c - 3 map1 . forEach ( function ( value , key ){ console . log ( key + " - " + value ) }) // a-1​ // b-2​ // c-3

我们也可以单独迭代键和值。

 for ( let values of map1 . values ()){ console . log ( values ) } //1 2 3 for ( let values of map1 . keys ()){ console . log ( values ) } //abc

这是对 Map 对象的快速概述。
让我知道您是否在实际应用中使用过它以及它的性能如何！



原文:

Let's learn about JavaScript Maps.

Maps are similar to objects.
They store the elements in a key/value pair.

However, a map can contain objects, functions, and other data types as a key, unlike objects.
We can create a Map using the new Map() constructor.

To insert it into the map, we use the set() method. We can use objects and functions as keys as well.

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
console.log(map1)
//expected output: Map {"a": 1, "b": 2}

To access the elements, we use the get() method. We call the get method on the key and get the corresponding values.

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
console.log(map1.get('a'));
// expected output: 1

To check if an element is present in the map, we get a function called has()

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
console.log(map1.has('c'))
//expected output: false

Then we have clear() and delete() methods which can enable us to remove data from the map

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
map1.delete('b');
console.log(map1)
//expected output: Map {"a" : 1}

To get the length of the Map we have the property of size which will give us the number of elements present on the map.

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
map1.set('c', 3);
console.log(map1.size); // expected output: 3

To iterate over the map, we can use for loops or for-each loop. The elements are accessed in the order they have been inserted.

const map1 = new Map(); map1.set('a', 1);
map1.set('b', 2);
map1.set('c', 3); for (let [key, value] of map1){ console.log(key, "-", value)
}
// a - 1
​// b - 2
​// c - 3 map1.forEach(function(value, key){ console.log(key + "-" + value)
})
// a-1
​// b-2
​// c-3

We can iterate over the keys and values individually as well.

for(let values of map1.values()){ console.log(values)
}
//1 2 3  for(let values of map1.keys()){ console.log(values)
}
//a b c

That was a quick overview of the Map object.
Let me know if you have used it in practical application and how did it perform!
item.author: sobhandash

item.title: 修复 Postman 桌面卡在 Windows 10 中加载 | Fix Postman Desktop Stuck on Loading in Windows 10
item.description:
我已经使用 Postman 将近一整年来做 API 测试，然后我遇到了这个问题。 当我打开笔记本电脑并打开 Postman 时，加载过程从未完成。

我立刻惊慌失措，因为我需要在一个团队项目上工作，需要共享 Postman 测试，这导致每个人都不得不使用 postman 作为工具来进行 API 测试。

我觉得很奇怪，因为以前从来没有这样，我想可能是因为网络问题。 我尝试更改互联网连接，但结果仍然相同。 然后我尝试重新启动笔记本电脑，但结果仍然相同。

我试图在谷歌上找到各种解决方案。 原来这是 Postman 用户的共同话题之一。 但在一些讨论中，我发现直到它能够解决问题才真正讨论（这让我更加恐慌）。 最后我找到了一种方法（对不起，我忘记了来源），在我尝试之后，它成功了。

该方法非常简单，即添加一个名为POSTMAN_DISABLE_GPU的环境变量并将其值设为false 。 之后重新启动邮递员，就完成了。

  1. 按开始（Windows）然后搜索Edit the system environment variables
  2. 然后单击底部的Environment Variables...按钮。
  3. 在System variables部分中，单击New...
  4. 用POSTMAN_DISABLE_GPU填充variable name名列，用false填充variable value列。
  5. 在所有窗口上按OK 。
  6. 重新启动 Postman 桌面客户端。
  7. 完毕。

经过进一步调查，事实证明这个问题随时可能出现意外。 这是关于GPU 兼容性的，但我不确定细节是什么。

这就是我在处理 Postman 在 Windows 10 上的加载屏幕上卡住的经验时可以分享的内容。感谢您将本文读到最后。 通过分享这篇文章来帮助其他有同样问题的人。



原文:

I've been using Postman for almost a full year to do API testing, then I came across this problem. When I turned on the laptop and opened Postman, the loading process never finished.

I immediately panicked because I had a need to work on a team project, where there was a need to share Postman tests, which resulted in everyone having to use postman as a tool to do API testing.

I feel weird because it's never been like this before, I think maybe because of internet problem. I tried changing the internet connection but the result is still the same. Then I tried restarting the laptop, but the result is still the same.

I tried to find various solutions on google. It turned out to be one of the common topics among Postman users. But in some discussions that I found not really discussing until it was able to solve the problem (and that made me even more panicked). Finally I found a way (sorry I forgot the source) and after I tried, it's worked.

The method is quite simple, namely by adding an environment variable with the name POSTMAN_DISABLE_GPU and giving it a value of false. After that restart postman, and it's done.

  1. Press start (windows) then search for Edit the system environment variables
  2. Then click the Environment Variables... button on bottom.
  3. In the System variables section, click New....
  4. Fill the variable name column with POSTMAN_DISABLE_GPU, and the variable value column with false.
  5. Press OK on all windows.
  6. Restart your Postman desktop client.
  7. DONE.

After further investigation, it turns out that this problem can occur at any time unexpectedly. It's about GPU compatibility, but I'm not sure what the details are.

That's what I can share regarding my experience dealing with Postman stuck on the loading screen on Windows 10. Thank you for reading this article to the end. Help others who have the same problem by share this article.
item.author: ivanauliaa

item.title: 如何使用 docker 和 SSL 在你的 Linux 服务器中部署 NextCloud | How to deploy NextCloud in your Linux Server with docker and SSL
item.description:
当您创建帐户并将文档、图像等存储在 Google 驱动器或 Dropbox 中时，您并不是数据的主人。 一般来说，我们说我们免费使用谷歌或微软的一个驱动器，但它不是免费的。 我们使用他们收集的个人数据为这些免费空间付费。 这就是 NextCloud 的用武之地。简单来说，Nextcloud 就是您可以控制的云基础设施。

它使您无论身在何处都可以访问所有文件。 它允许您共享和协作处理文档、发送和接收电子邮件、管理您的日历以及进行视频聊天。 您可以在您的 Linux 服务器上免费安装 Nextcloud 服务器软件，在您的 Windows、OS X 或 Linux 机器、Android 和 IOS 手机上安装客户端软件。

这里的主要缺点是您需要向 VPS 提供商支付 Linux 服务器的费用，并且您将负责服务器维护，除非您选择附带支持的 Nextcloud Enterprise。 例如，如果您选择 Contabo 供应商，您可以每月只需 6.99 美元即可拥有配备 200GB SSD、8GO RAM 和 4vCPU 的功能齐全的云解决方案。

安装 Nextcloud 前的先决条件

由于我们将使用 docker 部署 NextCloud 实例，因此您需要：

  • 具有 SSH 和 root 访问权限的 Linux 服务器
  • 安装在该服务器上的 Docker 和 docker-compose
  • 指向该服务器的域名

登录到您的 Linux 服务器并键入以下命令：

 mkdir nextcloud && cd nextcloud nano db.env

复制上述 GitHub gist 中db.env的内容，并将其粘贴到新创建的文件中。 在此之后，创建一个docker-compose.yml文件并将上述 Github gist 中的 docker-compose.yml 的内容复制并粘贴到其中。

注意：不要忘记将环境变量VIRTUAL_HOST和LETSENCRYPT_HOST更改为您的域名，将LETSENCRYPT_EMAIL 更改为您的电子邮件地址。

现在，是时候创建 docker 网络了，该网络将用于通过我们的域名将安全流量驱动到 Nextcloud 实例。

 docker network create nginx-proxy

然后让我们启动我们的 Nextcloud 实例。

 docker-compose up --build -d

我们的 Nextcloud 实例现在正在运行，但无法从 Internet 访问。 我们现在将配置 Nginx-proxy 以将流量驱动到我们的 Nextcloud 实例。 在使我们的实例完全正常运行后，我们将解释我们的 docker-compose 文件。

安装 Nginx-proxy 和 acme-compagnon

此容器安装在 docker 套接字上，以捕获 docker 创建的所有事件，以便能够代理任何具有 env 变量VIRTUAL_HOST定义的容器。 所有想要被 Nginx-proxy 代理的容器都必须与它连接到同一个网络。 想了解更多关于 Nginx-proxy 的信息，请访问项目的 GitHub 。

有关 acme-compagnon 的更多信息，请访问项目的 GitHub 。

我的存储库中有一个现成的 Nginx-proxy 模板。 您只需要克隆并运行它。 我也在我所有的项目中使用这个模板。 使用这种配置，可以在不到一分钟的时间内轻松完成工作。 只需使用以下命令。

 cd ~ git clone https://gitlab.com/tderick/nginx-proxy-conf.git docker-compose up --build -d

现在，您的 Nextcloud 实例正在运行，您可以通过您的域名访问它。

安装 Nextcloud 时，默认情况下它没有管理员帐户。 您需要创建它。 只需填写第一页上的表格并点击安装按钮。 不要将电子邮件作为用户名。 如果这样做，则会出现以下错误：

输入用户名和密码后，我们将到达以下页面，其中列出了要在我们的实例中安装的推荐应用程序。

我们可以看到有以下应用：

  • 日历
  • 联系人
  • 邮件
  • 在线编辑和协作

稍后我们将安装其他应用程序。 只需点击安装推荐的应用程序按钮。

我们的 nextcloud 实例现在已经安装并可以使用了。 现在，我们可以解释我们的 docker-compose 文件来理解这背后的魔力。

在这个 docker-compose 文件中，我们使用 3.9 版本，我们公开了三个服务、两个卷和一个默认的外部网络。

您可以选择自己喜欢的 DBMS。 我们选择使用 Postgres，因为它是一个非常强大的解决方案。

它是提供所有功能的官方 Nextcloud 容器。

包起来

在本教程中，我们将向您解释如何使用 docker-compose 在 Linux 服务器中部署 Nextcloud 实例，并使用 Let's Encrypt 颁发的免费 SSL 证书保护它。 您现在可以做的另一件事是在此旁边部署一个keycloak SSO解决方案，以在您的所有应用程序中集中身份验证。 这一步很简单，不影响之前的安装。 如果您有任何问题，请发表评论。

如果你喜欢这个教程，你可以给我买咖啡。 在接下来的教程中，我们将更多地探索 Nextcloud。



原文:

When you create your account and store your documents, images, etc in Google drive or dropbox, you are not the master of your data. Generally, we say that we use Google or Microsoft one drive for free but it’s not free. We pay for those free spaces with the personal data they collected. It’s where NextCloud comes in. In simple words, Nextcloud is your cloud infrastructure under your control.

What is NextCloud ?

NextCloud is open-source software that allows you to run your personnel cloud service like dropbox. It gives you access to all your files wherever you are. It allows you to share and collaborate on documents, send and receive email, manage your calendar and have video chats. You can install the Nextcloud server software free on your Linux server and the client’s software on your Windows, OS X, or Linux machine, Android, and IOS mobile phone.

The main drawback here is you need to pay for your Linux server to your VPS provider and you will be responsible for your server maintenance unless your choose Nextcloud Enterprise which comes with the support. For example, if you choose the Contabo provider, you can have your fully functional cloud solution with 200GB SSD, 8GO of RAM, and 4vCPU for only $6.99 a month.

Pre-requisite before the installation of Nextcloud

As we will deploy our NextCloud instance with docker, you need to have:

  • A Linux Server with SSH and root access
  • Docker and docker-compose installed on that server
  • A domain name pointed to that server

Deployment of NextCloud 

POSTGRES_PASSWORD=yourdbstrongpassword
POSTGRES_DB=nextcloud
POSTGRES_USER=nextcloud

db.env file

version: "3.9"

services:
  nextcloud_db:
    image: postgres:alpine
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - nextcloud_dbdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    env_file:
      - db.env

  redis:
    image: redis:alpine
    restart: always

  nextcloud_web:
    image: nextcloud:apache
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - nextcloud:/var/www/html
    environment:
      - VIRTUAL_HOST=cloud.yourdomain.com
      - LETSENCRYPT_HOST=cloud.yourdomain.com
      - LETSENCRYPT_EMAIL=yourmail # <===== For let's encrypt
      - POSTGRES_HOST=nextcloud_db
      - REDIS_HOST=redis
    env_file:
      - db.env
    depends_on:
      - nextcloud_db
      - redis

  cron:
    image: nextcloud:apache
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - nextcloud:/var/www/html
    entrypoint: /cron.sh
    depends_on:
      - nextcloud_db
      - redis

volumes:
  nextcloud_dbdata:
  nextcloud:

#Use this configuration in production with nginx-proxy container
networks:
  default:
    external:
      name: nginx-proxy

docker-compose.yml file

Login to your Linux server and type the following command:

mkdir nextcloud && cd nextcloud
nano db.env

Copie the content of the db.env in the above GitHub gist and paste it into the newly created file. After this, create a docker-compose.yml file and copy the content of the docker-compose.yml in the above Github gist and paste it into.

Note: Don’t forget the change the environment variables VIRTUAL_HOST and LETSENCRYPT_HOST with your domain name and LETSENCRYPT_EMAIL with your email address.

Now, it’s time to create the docker network that would be used to drive secure traffic to our Nextcloud instance through our domain name.

docker network create nginx-proxy

Then let’s start our Nextcloud instance.

docker-compose up --build -d

Our Nextcloud instance is now running but is not accessible from the internet. We will now configure Nginx-proxy to drive traffic to our Nextcloud instance. We will explain our docker-compose file after making our instance fully functional.

Installation of Nginx-proxy and acme-compagnon

Nginx proxy is a container running Nginx and docker-gen which is a service that generates reverse proxy configs for Nginx and reloads Nginx when containers are started or stopped.

This container is mounted on a docker socket to capture all events created by docker to be able to proxied any container with an env variable VIRTUAL_HOST define. All containers that want to be proxied by Nginx-proxy must be connected to the same network with it. To know more about Nginx-proxy, visit the GitHub of the project.

ACME-compagnon is a compagnon for Nginx-proxy responsible to automate the creation, renewal, and use of SSL certificates for proxied Docker containers through ACME protocol. For more information about acme-compagnon, visit the GitHub of the project.

I have a ready-to-use template for Nginx-proxy in my repository. You just need to clone and run it. I also use this template in all my projects. With this configuration, it’s easy to make things work in less than a minute. Just use the following command.

cd ~
git clone https://gitlab.com/tderick/nginx-proxy-conf.git 
docker-compose up --build -d

Now, your Nextcloud instance is running, and you can access it via your domain name.

When you install Nextcloud, it doesn’t come with an admin account by default. You need to create it. Just fill in the form on the first page and hit the install button. Don’t put email as username. if you do, you have the following error:

After putting in a username and password, we will arrive at the following page listing the recommended apps to install in our instance.

We can see there are applications for:

  • Calendar
  • Contacts
  • Mail
  • Online edition and collaboration

We will install other applications later. Just hit the install recommended apps button.

Our nextcloud instance is now installed and ready to use. Now, we can explain our docker-compose file to understand the magic behind this.

Docker-compose file explanation

In this docker-compose file, we use version 3.9 and we expose three services, two volumes, and one default external network.

nextcloud_db service

Nextcloud support multiple DBMS: MySQL, MariaDB, Oracle, PostgresSQL. It’s up to you to choose your favorite DBMS. We choose to use Postgres as it is a very powerful solution.

redis service

Redis is an excellent modern memory cache solution to use for distributed caching. It’s used by Nextcloud to significantly improve the Nextcloud server performance with memory caching where frequently-requested objects are stored for faster retrieval.

nextcloud_web service

It’s the official Nextcloud container with all the features offered.

cron service

Cron is a simple time-based job scheduler that runs small tasks on its own without the intervention of the user or the administrator. Cron is also an important part for Nextcloud to be running efficiently.

Wrap up

In this tutorial, we explain to you how to deploy your Nextcloud instance in your Linux server with docker-compose and secure it with a free SSL certificate issued by Let’s Encrypt. Another thing you can do now is to deploy a keycloak SSO solution next to this to centralize authentication among all your application. It’s pretty easy at this step and doesn’t affect the previous installation. If you have any questions, leave a comment.

If you like this tutorial, you can buy me coffee. In the upcoming tutorial, we will more explore Nextcloud.
item.author: tderick

item.title: 网络地址转换（NAT） | Network Address Translation(NAT)
item.description:
伊丽莎白·奥涅内奎

发表于网络内的计算机向 Internet 上的计算机发出请求。 该网络内的路由器识别该请求不是针对网络内的资源，因此路由器充当公共网络和专用网络之间的代理，然后将请求发送到防火墙。 防火墙在内部 IP 内看到来自计算机的请求，然后使用自己的公共 IP 地址向 Internet 发出相同的请求。 然后防火墙将来自 Internet 资源的响应返回到专用网络内的计算机。

从一个本地 IP 地址到一个全局 IP 地址。
  • 将由私有 IP 地址组成的整个 IP 地址空间隐藏在单个 IP 地址后面。
  • 到使用单个公共 IP 地址的大型专用网络。
  • 从本地 IP 地址到公共 IP 地址池。
  • 从全局 IP 地址到本地 IP 地址池。

    • 静态 NAT：这会将一个未注册的 IP 地址映射到

    注册的 IP 地址。

  • 动态 NAT：这会将未注册的 IP 地址转换为来自公共 IP 池的已注册 IP 地址

    地址。

  • 反向 NAT：这允许用户连接到自己
    使用互联网或公共网络。

  • 端口地址转换：也称为 NAT 过载或端口级复用 NAT 或单地址 NAT。 在这种情况下，许多本地（私有）IP 地址可以转换为

    一个注册的 IP 地址。

  • 重叠 NAT：当其网络使用相同 IP 地址的两个组织合并或当一个

    注册的 IP 地址被分配给多个设备。

    • 参考

    克里斯托弗罗德里格斯 - 斯特凡·斯米利科维奇—— May 13 5月13日



    原文:

    Elizabeth Onyenekwe

    Posted on May 14

    NAT is the process of mapping an internet protocol(ip) address to another so that a router can access resources outside a private network.

    How Does NAT Work ?

    NAT occurs between an internal IP and an external IP via a router.
    A computer inside a network makes a request to a computer on the internet. Routers within that network recognise that the request is not for a resource inside the network so the routers act as agents between the public network and the private network, then sends the request to the firewall. The firewall sees the request from the computer within the internal IP, then makes same request to the internet using its own public IP address. The firewall then returns the response from the internet resource to the computer inside the private network.

    Ways By Which NAT Can Map IP Addresses

    1. From a local IP address to one global IP address.
    2. Hiding an entire IP address space comprised of Private IP addresses behind a single IP address.
    3. To a large private network using a single public IP address.
    4. From a local IP address to a pool of public IP addresses.
    5. From a global IP address to a pool of local IP addresses.

    Types Of NAT

    1. Static NAT: This maps a single unregistered IP Address with

      a registered IP address.

  • Dynamic NAT: This translates an unregistered IP Address into a registered IP address from a pool of public IP

    addresses.

  • Reverse NAT: This allows users to connect to themselves
    using the internet or public network.

  • Port Address Translation: It is also known as NAT Overload or Port-level multiplexed NAT or Single Address NAT. In this, many local(private) IP addresses can be translated to

    a single registered IP address.

  • Overlapping NAT: This happens when two organisations whose networks uses the same IP address merge or when a

    registered IP address is assigned to multiple devices.

    • References

    Christopher Rodriguez - May 13

    Stefan Smiljkovic - May 13
item.author: elizabethonyen6

item.title: 我是如何学习 Git/Github 的基础知识的。 | How I learned the basics of Git/Github.
item.description:
我需要了解 Git 和 GitHub 的基础知识，所以我决定做一个简单的 Python 项目。 该项目是一个 CLI 刽子手游戏，可让您从一系列主题中进行选择。

我不想一开始就做一个非常困难的项目，因为我知道这不会帮助我很好地学习 Git 和 GitHub，因为我会迷失方向，并且大部分时间都不知道该做什么。

该项目在这里： https ://github.com/DempseyNewton/Hangman-Python。 我没有使用分支或类似的东西，因为我想将其保存到具有多种用途的项目中。

总之，这个项目确实帮助我理解和使用了 Git 和 GitHub 的绝对基础知识。



原文:

I needed to know the basics of Git and GitHub so I decided to do a simple Python project. The project is a CLI hangman game that lets you pick from a range of themes.

I didn't want to do a really hard project to start off with because I knew it wouldn't help me learn Git and GitHub very well since I would get lost and wouldn't know what to do most of the time.

The project is here: https://github.com/DempseyNewton/Hangman-Python. I didn't use branches or anything like that because I wanted to save that for a project that has a variety of uses.

In conclusion, this project really helped me understand and use the absolute basics of Git and GitHub.
item.author: dempsey

item.title: 🐉 AWS CDK 101 - 🏇 使用批处理 dynamodb 流删除另一个 dynamodb 表上的项目 | 🐉 AWS CDK 101 - 🏇 Using batched dynamodb stream to delete item on another dynamodb table
item.description:
如果万一错过了我之前的文章，请通过以下链接找到它。

在本文中，让我们向消息 dynamodb 表添加一个新的异步批处理集成，这将帮助我们从暂存 dynamodb 表中删除已处理的记录。

这种方法的好处💦

  • 在这种方法中，我们尝试使用直接调用 lambda 的 dynamodb 流异步清除临时表数据。
  • 因此，我们可以在可能无法直接在计算密集型工作负载中进行同步清理的系统中使用它。 通过避免这些 I/O 的等待时间来节省计算。
  • 同样，有些人可能会争辩说，无论如何我都在其他地方单独进行 lambda 调用，这也可以被认为是一种很好的解耦策略。
  • 但是调用费用和计算仍然适用。 然而，我们不需要更多的东西，流可以通过我们的 lambda 分批准备好，默认值为 100，所以一次调用。
  • 同时，处理程序可以在单个请求中执行批量请求以从 dynamodb 中删除数据。
  • 因此，您不仅可以读取一批流，还可以执行批量删除，当您进行此类受限制的异步集成时，这速度很快，并且可以很好地减少 dynamodb 表中写入单元的使用。
  • 也许我稍后会单独写一篇关于它的文章，暂时不限于这里的 1:1 删除操作。

计划🐞

在这里，我们将使用 dynamodb 流通过调用实现我们目标的新 lambda 处理程序函数来触发deleteItem操作。

建设💮

我们需要一个新的 lambda 函数代码，它有一个新的处理程序来接收 dynamodb 流事件对象并对其进行处理，如下所示。

在文件lambda/message-stream.ts中创建一个新的 lambda 函数。

在这里，您可能会发现我们将事件名称定位为INSERT ，同样我们可以在这些流调用期间更好地控制我们想要的结果，如下所示。

新的句柄函数逻辑 stuffed_flatbread：

 exports . created = async function ( event : any ) { console . log ( " Received stream: " , JSON . stringify ( event , undefined , 2 )); const results : any [] = []; await Promise . all ( event . Records . map ( async ( Record : any ) => { console . log ( JSON . stringify ( Record , undefined , 2 )); if ( Record . eventName === " INSERT " ) { results . push ( await deleteDbItem ( Record . dynamodb . Keys )); } }) ); results . map (( res ) => console . log ( res )); };

辅助函数 dynamodb deleteItem 💐

从 dynamodb 表中执行 deleteItem 的简单辅助函数。

 const deleteDbItem : any = async ( keys : any ) => { console . log ( " Deleting: " , { keys }); const deleteData : DeleteItemInput = { TableName : process . env . STAGING_MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME || "" , Key : keys , ReturnConsumedCapacity : " TOTAL " , }; console . log ( " deleteItem: " , JSON . stringify ( deleteData , undefined , 2 )); return await dynamo . deleteItem ( deleteData ). promise (); };

对 dynamodb 表定义的小改动🍊

我已经强调了必要的更改，我们需要为我们的表执行 dynamodb 流生成。

最重要的是，我已经请求了新旧图像，它们将包含所有必要的数据，但是，我们不会使用所有数据。 这仅用于演示目的。

 const messages = new dynamodb . Table ( this , " MessagesTable " , { tableName : process . env . messagesTable , sortKey : { name : " createdAt " , type : dynamodb . AttributeType . NUMBER }, partitionKey : { name : " messageId " , type : dynamodb . AttributeType . STRING }, encryption : dynamodb . TableEncryption . AWS_MANAGED , readCapacity : 5 , writeCapacity : 5 , //New item added below stream : dynamodb . StreamViewType . NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES });

定义新的 lambda 🍇

在这里，我们将使用上面使用的代码块来配置我们的 lambda，如下所示在我们的主堆栈中。

 const messageStreamFunc = new lambda . Function ( this , " messageStreamFunc " , { runtime : lambda . Runtime . NODEJS_14_X , code : lambda . Code . fromAsset ( " lambda " ), handler : " message-stream.created " , logRetention : logs . RetentionDays . ONE_MONTH , tracing : Tracing . ACTIVE , layers : [ nodejsUtils , nodejsXray ], environment : { STAGING_MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME : envParams . messages . stgTableName || "" , }, }); messageStreamFunc . applyRemovalPolicy ( RemovalPolicy . DESTROY );

授予处理程序写入表的权限💨

此外，我们的处理程序必须具有足够的权限才能从其他 dynamodb 表stgMessages中删除。

 stgMessages . grantWriteData ( messageStreamFunc );

将事件源添加到 lambda 函数🌽

现在是时候不要将处理函数连接到 dynamodb 事件源了，如下所示。

 messageStreamFunc . addEventSource ( new DynamoEventSource ( messages , { startingPosition : lambda . StartingPosition . LATEST , }) )

或者您可以在几秒钟内改进批量大小和窗口以进行长轮询，如下所示。

 messageStreamFunc . addEventSource ( new DynamoEventSource ( messages , { startingPosition : lambda . StartingPosition . LATEST , batchSize : 100 , maxBatchingWindow : Duration . seconds ( 60 ) }))

示例 dynamodb 流对象🥣

我已经共享了用作有效负载的 dynamodb 流对象，以在下面调用我们的处理程序 lambda。


{ "Records" : [ { "eventID" : "5c9aa5395f970324e088a32578ee0a66" , "eventName" : "INSERT" , "eventVersion" : "1.1" , "eventSource" : "aws:dynamodb" , "awsRegion" : "ap-south-1" , "dynamodb" : { "ApproximateCreationDateTime" : 1652355068 , "Keys" : { "createdAt" : { "N" : "1652355039000" }, "messageId" : { "S" : "47d97141-01e7-42a1-b6b3-0c59a6a3827e" } }, "NewImage" : { "createdAt" : { "N" : "1652355039000" }, "messageId" : { "S" : "47d97141-01e7-42a1-b6b3-0c59a6a3827e" }, "event" : { "S" : "{ \n \" message \" : { \" new \" : \" A secret message \" } \n }" } }, "SequenceNumber" : "20927100000000035334874026" , "SizeBytes" : 173 , "StreamViewType" : "NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES" }, "eventSourceARN" : "arn:aws:dynamodb:ap-south-1:8888888:table/MessagesTable/stream/2022-05-*****" } ]
}

执行期间的控制台日志🍿

最后在执行后，我们可以在event对象中找到我们收到的上述 JSON 有效负载，然后将其用于从我们的暂存表中删除，您可以在云观察日志中找到以下结果。

在下一篇文章中，我们将演示如何使用类似的方法从 S3 中删除我们之前创建的 Object。

我们将向我们的堆栈添加更多连接，并通过创建新结构使其在即将发布的文章中更有用，因此请考虑关注并订阅我的时事通讯。

如果您想☕ 给我买杯咖啡，帮助我努力，那就太好了。

🔁 Original post at 🔗 Dev Post 🔁 🔗 Dev Post的原始帖子

🔁 Reposted at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber 🔁 在 🔗 dev 转发到@aravindvcyber



原文:

🔰 Beginners new to AWS CDK, please do look at my previous articles one by one in this series.

If in case missed my previous article, do find it with the below links.

🔁 Original previous post at 🔗 Dev Post

🔁 Reposted previous post at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber

In this article, let us add a new async batch integration to our message dynamodb table which will help us to delete the processed records from the staging dynamodb table.

Benefits in this approach 💦

  • In this approach we tried to clear the staging table data, asynchronously using the dynamodb stream directly invoking a lambda.
  • So we can use this in systems where we may not directly do the scavenging synchronous in a compute-intensive workload. Saving compute by avoiding wait time for these I/O.
  • Again, some may argue that anyway I am making a lambda call elsewhere separately, which could be thought of as a good decoupling strategy as well.
  • But invocation charges and compute still apply there. Yet, we need no one more thing that the streams can be ready in batches by our lambda and the default is 100, so one invocation.
  • At the same time, the handler can do a batch request to delete the data from the dynamodb in a single request.
  • So not only you can read a batch of streams, but you can also perform batch delete which, is fast as well as a good reduction in usage of write units in dynamodb table when you have such throttled async integrations.
  • Maybe I will write a separate article later about it, for now, it is not limited to the 1:1 delete operation here.

Planning 🐞

Here we will be making use of the dynamodb streams to trigger the deleteItem action by invoking a new lambda handler function which achieves our objective.

Construction 💮

We need a new lambda function code that has a fresh handler to receive the dynamodb stream event object and process it as shown below.

Create a new lambda function in the file lambda/message-stream.ts.

Here you may find that we are targeting an event name to be INSERT, likewise we can have finer control over our desired outcome during these stream invocations as shown below.

New handle function logic stuffed_flatbread: 

exports.created = async function (event: any) { console.log("Received stream:", JSON.stringify(event, undefined, 2)); const results: any[] = []; await Promise.all( event.Records.map(async (Record: any) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(Record, undefined, 2)); if (Record.eventName === "INSERT") { results.push(await deleteDbItem(Record.dynamodb.Keys)); } }) ); results.map((res) => console.log(res));
};

Helper function dynamodb deleteItem 💐

Simple helper function to perform deleteItem from a dynamodb table.

const deleteDbItem: any = async (keys: any) => { console.log("Deleting: ", { keys }); const deleteData: DeleteItemInput = { TableName: process.env.STAGING_MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME || "", Key: keys, ReturnConsumedCapacity: "TOTAL", }; console.log("deleteItem: ", JSON.stringify(deleteData, undefined, 2)); return await dynamo.deleteItem(deleteData).promise();
};

Minor changes to the dynamodb table definition 🍊

I have highlighted the necessary changes, we need to perform dynamodb stream generation for our table.

Most importantly, I have requested both the new and old images, which will have all the necessary data, however, we are not going to use all of the data. This is only for demonstration purposes.

const messages = new dynamodb.Table(this, "MessagesTable", { tableName: process.env.messagesTable, sortKey: { name: "createdAt", type: dynamodb.AttributeType.NUMBER }, partitionKey: { name: "messageId", type: dynamodb.AttributeType.STRING }, encryption: dynamodb.TableEncryption.AWS_MANAGED, readCapacity: 5, writeCapacity: 5, //New item added below stream: dynamodb.StreamViewType.NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
});

Defining the new lambda 🍇

Here we will use the code bloc used above to provision our lambda as shown below inside our master stack.

const messageStreamFunc = new lambda.Function(this, "messageStreamFunc", { runtime: lambda.Runtime.NODEJS_14_X, code: lambda.Code.fromAsset("lambda"), handler: "message-stream.created", logRetention: logs.RetentionDays.ONE_MONTH, tracing: Tracing.ACTIVE, layers: [nodejsUtils,nodejsXray], environment: { STAGING_MESSAGES_TABLE_NAME: envParams.messages.stgTableName || "", },
});
messageStreamFunc.applyRemovalPolicy(RemovalPolicy.DESTROY);

Grant permission for the handler to write to the table 💨

Also, our handler must have sufficient access to delete from the other dynamodb table stgMessages.

stgMessages.grantWriteData(messageStreamFunc);

Adding Event source to the lambda function 🌽

It is time not to connect the handler function to the dynamodb event source as follows.

messageStreamFunc.addEventSource( new DynamoEventSource(messages, { startingPosition: lambda.StartingPosition.LATEST, })
)

or you can improve the batch-size and window in seconds for long polling as follows.

messageStreamFunc.addEventSource(new DynamoEventSource(messages, { startingPosition: lambda.StartingPosition.LATEST, batchSize:100, maxBatchingWindow: Duration.seconds(60)
}))

Sample dynamodb stream object 🥣

I have shared the dynamodb stream object used as payload to invoke our handler lambda below.


{ "Records": [ { "eventID": "5c9aa5395f970324e088a32578ee0a66", "eventName": "INSERT", "eventVersion": "1.1", "eventSource": "aws:dynamodb", "awsRegion": "ap-south-1", "dynamodb": { "ApproximateCreationDateTime": 1652355068, "Keys": { "createdAt": { "N": "1652355039000" }, "messageId": { "S": "47d97141-01e7-42a1-b6b3-0c59a6a3827e" } }, "NewImage": { "createdAt": { "N": "1652355039000" }, "messageId": { "S": "47d97141-01e7-42a1-b6b3-0c59a6a3827e" }, "event": { "S": "{\n \"message\": {\"new\": \"A secret message\"}\n}" } }, "SequenceNumber": "20927100000000035334874026", "SizeBytes": 173, "StreamViewType": "NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES" }, "eventSourceARN": "arn:aws:dynamodb:ap-south-1:8888888:table/MessagesTable/stream/2022-05-*****" } ]
}

Console log during execution 🍿

Finally post-execution, we could find the above JSON payload we have received in the event object and which is then used to delete from our staging table you may find the results below in cloud watch logs.

In the next article, we will demonstrate how we will use a similar approach to delete Object from S3, which we have previously created.

We will be adding more connections to our stack and making it more usable in the upcoming articles by creating new constructs, so do consider following and subscribing to my newsletter.

⏭ We have our next article in serverless, do check out

🎉 Thanks for supporting! 🙏

Would be great if you like to ☕ Buy Me a Coffee, to help boost my efforts.

🔁 Original post at 🔗 Dev Post

🔁 Reposted at 🔗 dev to @aravindvcyber
item.author: aravindvcyber

item.title: 如何从 Web2 过渡到 Web3 | How to Transition from Web2 to Web3
item.description:
你好呀！ 我的名字是 Dany，我是一名全栈 Web 开发人员，目前正在从 Web2 过渡到 Web3。 我的目标是全职从事 Web3 工作，并把我所有的时间都用于让 Web 更加去中心化。

我在这段旅程中已经有大约 4 个月的时间了，想和你分享我的故事和我的建议。 你可能听说过这个叫做 Web3 的东西，关于去中心化、加密、区块链和 NFT。 也许您还问自己“我应该进入 Web3”或“我如何才能进入 Web3”。

这些是我现在想在这里回答的问题。

重要的是要注意，本指南特别适用于已经在技术领域并希望过渡的人。 对于想开始从事技术工作的人，我将来会单独发一篇文章！

议程

  • 找个理由
  • 社区是关键
  • 学习基础知识
  • 资源
  • 构建项目

对我来说，这是最重要的一步。 如果你找到一个理由，你将永远不会失去动力。 在一个每天都在变化的空间里，你需要动力。 你上周学到的东西现在可能已经过时了。 如果你缺乏动力，这可能会让你崩溃。

我的动机是去中心化。 我的目标是完全去中心化我们的网络，原因很简单。 我希望互联网由我们、用户运营，而不是由大型科技公司和政府运营。 有一天你上传了一个视频，第二天你就被禁止了，你的账户也被删除了。 即使没有理由，他们也可以这样做。 他们拥有您和我们的数据。

我们才刚刚开始，我们还有很多悬而未决的问题，比如如何防止用户通过中央授权上传有害内容。 我很乐观，我们会在未来找到解决方案，因为我在这个领域看到了很多优秀和有才华的人。 这是关于用户的。

社区是关键

在每个领域都是如此。 我认为在 Web3 中尤其如此。 在我完全投入 Web3 之前的几周，我开始每天使用 Twitter。 这是我迄今为止做出的最好的决定之一。 我在那里遇到了很多善良，乐于助人和有才华的人！ 我今天称为朋友的人。

你必须知道我是一个更内向的人，但在科技推特上，我喜欢与人互动和交谈。 我知道 Twitter 可能非常有毒，但 Tech Twitter 是 100% 棒极了。 每个人都试图帮助、激励、教导和传播快乐。

尽管 Web3 相当新，但您已经可以找到许多可以学习的资源。 这对我来说是压倒性的。 哪一个是最好的？ 哪一个适合我？

学习平台和社区

然后我找到了 LearnWeb3DAO（Twitter： @LearnWeb3DAO ）。 我无法表达我有多高兴找到这个平台和这个社区。

首先，它们提供了一个平台，您可以在其中学习 Web3，从最开始到非常高级的主题。 一切都在你的步伐。

他们的网站： https ://www.learnweb3.io/

现在，他们提供 4 个核心曲目：

  • 大一新生
  • 二年级
  • 初级
  • 高级的

这正是我所寻找的。 从一开始就有结构化的学习路径。

每个轨道都有多个级别，您可以在其中学习区块链的核心概念，它们还为您提供了很好的资源。

最重要的是，您可以访问他们的 Discord 服务器。 这是我进入 Web3 时所做的最好的事情。 在撰写本文时，我们已经达到了 19.000 多名会员。 有成千上万的人正在学习 Web3，有些人甚至已经在这个行业工作。 每当您遇到困难并有疑问时，都会有人可以帮助您。

此外，我们每个月都会举办多个活动和社区电话会议。 例如，上个月我们举办了一个活动，多人向我们展示了他们的项目。 或者我们有一个赠品活动，人们可以赢取奖品。 但最重要的部分是人。 当我说它真的像一个家庭时，请相信我。

我怎么强调加入和参与社区的重要性都不为过。

当然，您可以自己做所有事情，并且您可能会成功。 但这需要更长的时间，会更难，而且你不会有这么多的乐趣。 社区是关键！

学习基础知识

如果您是一名开发人员并且熟悉 HTML、CSS 和 JS，那么您已经成功了一半！ 这是一个补充。 这就像 Typescript 和 Javascript。 在这种情况下真的很神奇，改变世界的功能。

有很多很棒的资源可以用来学习 Web 开发基础知识和更多关于它的博客文章。 我们生活在一个您可以仅从互联网上了解发展的世界。 那是免费的。 如果有一门课程我会推荐从头开始学习 Web 开发，那就是：
区块链和 Solidity

现在是时候深入研究区块链开发了。 你可能听说过以太坊，它肯定是最著名的区块链。 这是我建议开始的一个。 生态系统很棒，你会找到大量的资源。 社区很大而且非常好。

对于以太坊，你需要学习 Solidity。 即使您不想自己编写智能合约，了解幕后发生的事情也非常重要。 在我看来，学习编程语言的最好方法是通过编码。

多么酷啊？ 它写得非常好，易于理解，您可以通过构建游戏来练习。

资源

您可以使用大量资源来了解 Web3 并深入了解它。 我想你已经可以想象我的第一资源是什么了。 是的！ 它的LearnWeb3DAO。 我想我已经说过为什么它很棒了。 但我们还有更多！

以下是一些出色的视频内容创作者：

帕特里克·柯林斯 ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/PatrickCollins

智能合约程序员⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJWh7F3AFyQ_x01VKzr9eyA

链环⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/chainlink

炼金术⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/AlchemyPlatform

奥斯汀格里菲斯⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_HI2i2peo1A-STdG22GFsA

白板加密⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/WhiteboardCrypto

纳德达比特⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/naderdabit

推特也很棒。 尤其是科技推特。 尤其是 Web3 技术推特！

这里有一些您可以关注的非常好的 Web3 内容创建者。 它们都提供了持续而优质的内容：

参数=> 每日 Web3 内容，惊人的线程

我可以在这里列出 100 多人，但现在，这应该是一个很好的起点。 如果您在这个泡泡中活跃，您将立即与所有其他人联系！

构建项目

作为开发人员，最好的学习方式是实际构建项目。 您将在 LearnWeb3DAO 的 Tracks 中执行此操作，但您也应该自己构建一些东西。 如果您浏览 Youtube，您会发现很多很多教程，您将在其中构建 NFT 市场、简单的代币、NFT 集合、DAO 等。

一开始，这非常好，因为你会学到很多东西，因为创建者会解释代码和发生了什么。

但不要陷入教程地狱。 自己建造东西。

许多人（包括我自己）一开始都在努力构建一个没有教程的项目。 这是完全正常的。 我的一个建议是学习教程，在完成教程项目后，您可以使用自己的功能对其进行扩展。

您将获得自信，这将对您有很大帮助，同时完全靠自己构建一些东西。
你不必发明轮子。 只需制作多个有趣的小项目或一个您热爱的更大的项目。

慢慢来

您不必费尽心思，辞掉工作，直接跳入 Web3。 慢慢开始，并参与 Twitter 和 Discord。 只是好奇，总是愿意学习新东西。 每一天。 这需要时间。 有些人每天可以投入一小时，有些人可以每天投入 8 小时。 我有一份全职工作和一个家庭。 我每天早上都起得很早（周末也是如此），花时间学习和构建 Web3，并在 Twitter 和 Discord 上与人们互动。

每个人都有不同的情况。 找到你的方式，明智地利用你的时间，你就会进入这个行业。 迟早。



原文:

Hello there! My name is Dany and I'm a Full Stack Web Developer and currently transitioning from Web2 to Web3. My goal is to work in Web3 Full-Time and put all my time to make the Web more decentralized.

I'm on this journey for about 4 months now and want to share my story and my tips with you. You may heard of this thing called Web3, about decentralization, Crypto, Blockchain, and NFTs. Maybe you also ask yourself “Should I get into Web3” or “How i can get into Web3”.

These are the questions I want to answer right here, right now.

It's important to note that this guide is especially for people that are already in the tech space and want to transition. For people that want to get started in tech, I will do a separate post in the future!

Agenda

  • Find a reason
  • Community is key
  • Learn the fundamentals
  • Resources
  • Build Projects

For me, this is the most important step. If you find a reason you will never lose motivation. And you will need Motivation in a space that changes every single day. Web3 is still very early. Something you learned last week could be obsolete by now. If you lack motivation this could break you.

My motivation is decentralization. My goal is to fully decentralize our web for one simple reason. I want that the internet is run by us, the users, and not by big tech companies and the government. Platforms like YouTube, TikTok, etc. can do whatever they want with your account. One day you upload a video, the next day you are banned and your account is deleted. They can do this even without a reason. They own you and our data.

Web3 wants to change this. Web3 is a vision that the users own their data and no one is excluded. We are just at the beginning and we have many open questions, like how to prevent users to upload harmful content with a central authority. I'm optimistic that we will find a solution for this in the future because I see so many nice and talented people in this space. Web3 isn't about money or scam. It's about the user.

Community is key

This is true in every field. I think it's especially true in Web3. A few weeks before I dived into Web3 completely I started to use Twitter on a daily basis. It was one of the best decisions I made so far. I met so many nice, helpful and talented people out there! People that I call friends today.

You have to know I'm more of an introverted person but on Tech Twitter I love to engage and talk to people. I know that Twitter can be very toxic but Tech Twitter is 100% awesome. Everyone tries to help, to motivate, teach, and to spread happiness.

Although Web3 is pretty new you can already find many resources from where you can learn. It was overwhelming for me. Which one is the best? Which one suits me?

Learning Platform and a community

Then i found LearnWeb3DAO (Twitter: @LearnWeb3DAO). I can't express how happy I am to found this platform and this community.

First of all, they offer a platform where you can learn Web3, from the very beginning to very advanced topics. All on your pace.

Their website: https://www.learnweb3.io/

Right now they offer their 4 core tracks:

  • Freshman
  • Sophomore
  • Junior
  • Senior

That was exactly what I searched for. A structured learning path from the very beginning.

Each track has multiple levels where you learn the core concepts of Blockchain and they also provide nice resources for you.

On top of that, you can access their Discord Server. And that was the best thing I did while getting into Web3. At the time of writing this, we have reached over 19.000 Members. There are thousands of people that are learning Web3 and some even already working in this industry. Whenever you are stuck and have questions there will be someone who can help you.

Also, we have multiple events and community calls each month. For example last month we had an event where multiple people showed us their projects. Or we had a Giveaway Event where people can win prizes. But the most important part is the people. Believe me when I say that it's really like a family.

I can't emphasize enough how important it is to join and participate in a community.

Sure you could do everything on your own and you may succeed. But it will take longer, it will be harder and you will not have this much fun. Community is key!

Learn the fundamentals

If you are a developer and familiar with HTML, CSS, and JS you are halfway there! Web3 is not something that is completely new and will replace Web2. It's an addition. It's like Typescript and Javascript. Typescript adds nice features. In this case really amazing, world-changing features.

There are many great resources out there to learn Web Development fundamentals and more blog posts about it. We live in a world where you can learn about development just from the internet. And that for free. If there is one course I would recommend for learning Web Development from scratch it would be that:
https://www.udemy.com/course/the-complete-web-developer-zero-to-mastery/

Blockchain and Solidity

Now it's time to dive into Blockchain development. You may heard of Ethereum and it's for sure the most well-known Blockchain. And it's the one I would suggest to start with. The ecosystem is fantastic and you will find a ton of resources for it. The community is quite big and really nice.

For Ethereum, you need to learn Solidity. Even if you don't want to write Smart Contracts on your own it's really important to understand what's happening under the hood. In my opinion, the best way to learn a programming language is through coding.

https://cryptozombies.io/ is a website where you will learn basic and advanced Solidity by building a Zombie Game. How cool is that? It's really well written, easy to understand and you can practice by building a game.

Resources

There are a ton of resources you can use to learn about Web3 and get into it. I guess you can already imagine what is my number one resource. Yes! Its LearnWeb3DAO. I think I already said why it's awesome. But we have more!

Here are some great video content creators:

Patrick Collins ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/PatrickCollins

Smart Contract Programmer ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJWh7F3AFyQ_x01VKzr9eyA

Chainlink ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/chainlink

Dapp University ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/DappUniversity

Alchemy ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/AlchemyPlatform

Blockgeeks ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/BlockGeeks

EatTheBlocks ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/EatTheBlocks

Austin Griffith ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_HI2i2peo1A-STdG22GFsA

Whiteboard Crypto ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/WhiteboardCrypto

Nader Dabit ⇒ https://www.youtube.com/c/naderdabit

Twitter is also awesome. Especially Tech Twitter. And especially Web3 Tech Twitter!

Here are some very nice Web3 content creators you can follow. They all provide constant and good content:

Abel => Awesome content, amazing threads

Kacie => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Haardik => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Sneh => One of LearnWeb3DAO founders, working in Web3

Peter => Daily Web3 content and an amazing person

Oscar => Provides Web3 content on a daily basis and explains it very well

Balddev => I love his content, always about Web3 and how to create a better web

Nader Dabit => A legend, already working in Web3 for me a must-follow

Vitto => Super sympathetic guy, also working in Web3

Rahat => Amazing Web3 and mostly Polygon Content

Anish => Talented young developer and share his learnings

Param => Daily Web3 Content, amazing threads

Suhail => Amazing young developer, Solidity Pro

Francesco => His threads are awesome, Web3 enthusiasts and Docker Captain

Etisha => Amazing content, super sympathetic, know how to provide value

Krishna => Provides amazing Web3 content

I could list over 100 people here but for now, this should be a good starting point. And if you are active in this bubble you will connect with all the others in no time!

Build Projects

As developers, the best way to learn is to actually build projects. You will do this in the Tracks of LearnWeb3DAO but you should also build something on your own. If you go through Youtube you will find many, many tutorials where you will build NFT Marketplaces, a simple Token, NFT Collection, DAOs, etc.

In the beginning, this is excellent because you will learn a lot because the creator will explain the code and what's going on.

But don't get stuck in tutorial hell. Build things on your own.

Many people (including myself) are struggling, in the beginning, to build a project without a tutorial. That's totally normal. One tip from me would be to take tutorials and after you finished the tutorial project you extend it with your own features.

You will gain self-confidence and that will help you a lot while building something completely on your own.
You don't have to invent the wheel. Just make multiple small fun projects or maybe one bigger project that you are passionate about.

Take your time

You don't have to make a hard cut, quit your job, and jump right into Web3. Begin slowly, and engage on Twitter and Discord. Just be curious and always willing to learn new stuff. Every single day. And this will take time. Some people can invest one hour a day, some can put in 8 hours a day. I have a Full-Time Job and a family. I stand up very early every morning (Also at the weekends), to invest time to learn and build in Web3 and to engage with people on Twitter and Discord.

Everyone has different circumstances. Find your way, use your time wisely and you will get your foot into this industry. Sooner or later.
item.author: danytulumidis

item.title: .NET MAUI（跨平台框架） | .NET MAUI (Cross Platform framework)
item.description:
使用 .NET MAUI 我们可以从一个共享的代码库开发可以在移动（Android、iOS）和桌面（macOS、Windows）上运行的应用程序。

但是，.NET MAUI 可以使用单个项目创建多平台应用程序。 由于不同的操作系统和设备规格，创建跨平台应用程序并非易事。 可以针对 Android、iOS、macOS 和 Windows 的单个共享项目。

  • 用于运行 .NET MAUI 应用程序的简化调试目标选择。
  • 单个项目中的共享资源文件。
  • 指定应用标题、ID 和版本的单个应用清单。
  • 在需要时访问特定于平台的 API 和工具。
  • 单个跨平台应用程序入口点。

    • 结论
    我对 .NET MAUI 以及它将带来的所有生产力和性能感到非常兴奋。 我期待使用 .NET MAUI 构建应用程序，并与大家分享我的学习和经验。

    谢谢阅读！
    随时欢迎反馈。



    原文:

    .NET MAUI stands for .NET Multi-platform App UI.

    .NET MAUI is a first class cross-platform framework for creating native mobile and desktop apps with C# and XAML.

    Using .NET MAUI We can develop apps that can run on Mobile (Android, iOS) and Desktop (macOS, Windows) from a single shared code-base.

    .NET MAUI is open source and is the evolution of Xamarin.Forms.

    .NET MAUI and Xamarin.Forms has many similarities. Xamarin.Forms need separate project for each platform. However, .NET MAUI can create multi platform apps using a single project. One of the key aim of .NET MAUI is to implement your app logic and UI layout in single code-base. Also .NET MAUI allow you to add platform-specific code and resources if necessary.

    Creating a cross-platform app is not easy task due to deferent OS and device specs. .NET MAUI simplified it by abstracts them into a single shared project that can target Android, iOS, macOS, and Windows.

    .NET MAUI single project provides the following features:

    • A single shared project that can target Android, iOS, macOS, and Windows.
    • A simplified debug target selection for running your .NET MAUI apps.
    • Shared resource files within the single project.
    • A single app manifest that specifies the app title, id, and version.
    • Access to platform-specific APIs and tools when required.
    • A single cross-platform app entry point.

    Conclusion
    I am very excited about .NET MAUI and all the productivity and performance it will bring. I look forward to build apps using .NET MAUI and share my learnings and experiences with you all.

    Thanks for reading!
    Feedback is always welcome.
item.author: nalinj

item.title: 如何在 JavaScript 中计算两个日期之间的天数差 | How to calculate the difference of days between two dates in JavaScript
item.description:
你有没有试过在没有库的情况下在 JavaScript 中计算两个日期之间的天数？ 好吧，这就是方法

简短的回答：

 const firstDate = new Date ( " 05/10/2022 " ) // 10th May, 2022 const secondDate = new Date () // today, 14th May, 2022 const millisecondsDiff = secondDate . getTime () - firstDate . getTime () const daysDiff = Math . round ( millisecondsDiff / ( 24 * 60 * 60 * 60 ) )

长答案

要找出两个日期之间的差异，首先需要获取以毫秒为单位的时间值。 您可以使用Date实例上的getTime方法获得此信息：

 const firstDate = new Date ( " 05/10/2022 " ) // 10th May, 2022 const secondDate = new Date () // today, 14th May, 2022 const firstDateInMs = firstDate . getTime () const secondDateInMs = secondDate . getTime ()

毫秒值是一个数字，您可以在该数字上执行减法等算术运算。

下一步是找出两个日期之间的差异：

 const differenceBtwDates = secondDateInMs - firstDateInMs console . log ( differenceBtwDates ) // 351628869

现在，两个日期之间的差异以毫秒为单位。 这是您将毫秒转换为天的地方。 怎么做？

首先，您以毫秒为单位获得 1 天（24 小时）的等价物。 就是这样：

 const aDayInMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 console . log ( aDayInMs ) // 86400000
  • 接下来的 60 代表 60 秒，也就是一分钟
  • 现在您有了一天的毫秒值，您可以使用它来确定日期之间的差异天数。

    如果86400000 ( aDayInMs ) 毫秒为一天，那么351628869 ( differenceBtwDates ) 毫秒为：

     const daysDiff = differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs console . log ( daysDiff ) // 4.0782153125

    由于它返回一个浮点数，您可以使用Math.round()将其四舍五入：

     const daysDiff = Math . round ( differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs ) console . log ( daysDiff ) // 4

    对比 5 月 10 日和 5 月 14 日，相差 4 天。

    这是完整的代码：

     const firstDate = new Date ( " 05/10/2022 " ) // 10th May, 2022 const secondDate = new Date () // today, 14th May, 2022 const firstDateInMs = firstDate . getTime () const secondDateInMs = secondDate . getTime () const differenceBtwDates = secondDateInMs - firstDateInMs const aDayInMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 const daysDiff = Math . round ( differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs ) console . log ( daysDiff ) // 4

    我使用 5 月 10 日和 14 日进行解释。



原文:

Have you ever tried calculating the number of days between two dates in JavaScript without a library? Well, here's how

Short answer: 

const firstDate = new Date("05/10/2022") // 10th May, 2022
const secondDate = new Date() // today, 14th May, 2022 const millisecondsDiff = secondDate.getTime() - firstDate.getTime() const daysDiff = Math.round( millisecondsDiff / (24 * 60 * 60 * 60)
)

Long answer

To find the difference between two dates, you first need to get the time value in milliseconds. You get this using the getTime method on the Date instance:

const firstDate = new Date("05/10/2022") // 10th May, 2022
const secondDate = new Date() // today, 14th May, 2022 const firstDateInMs = firstDate.getTime()
const secondDateInMs = secondDate.getTime()

The millisecond value is a number on which you can then perform arithmetic operations like subtractions.

The next step is finding the difference between both dates:

const differenceBtwDates = secondDateInMs - firstDateInMs console.log(differenceBtwDates)
// 351628869

Now, you have the difference in milliseconds between both dates. This is where you convert the milliseconds to days. How to do that?

First, you get the equivalence of 1 day (24 hours) in milliseconds. Here's how:

const aDayInMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 console.log(aDayInMs)
// 86400000
  • 24 stands for 24 hours
  • 60 stands for 60 minutes, which makes an hour, thereby converting 24 hours to minutes
  • the next 60 stands for 60 seconds, which makes a minute
  • and 1000 stands for 1000 milliseconds, which makes a second

Now that you have the milliseconds value for a day, you can use it to determine how many days the difference between the dates are.

If 86400000 (aDayInMs) milliseconds makes a day, then, 351628869 (differenceBtwDates) milliseconds makes:

const daysDiff = differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs console.log(daysDiff)
// 4.0782153125

As it returns a floating number, you can round it up with Math.round():

const daysDiff = Math.round(differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs) console.log(daysDiff)
// 4

Comparing May 10th and May 14th, it is a 4-day difference.

Here's the full code:

const firstDate = new Date("05/10/2022") // 10th May, 2022
const secondDate = new Date() // today, 14th May, 2022 const firstDateInMs = firstDate.getTime()
const secondDateInMs = secondDate.getTime() const differenceBtwDates = secondDateInMs - firstDateInMs const aDayInMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 const daysDiff = Math.round(differenceBtwDates / aDayInMs) console.log(daysDiff)
// 4

firstDate and secondDate can be any dates at all. I used May 10th and 14th for explanation purposes.
item.author: dillionmegida

item.title: 简单的无服务器调度程序 | Simple Serverless Scheduler
item.description:
当我为我的应用程序CloudPouch 开发许可证密钥管理解决方案时，我不得不面对延迟取消付费订阅的问题。

当用户出于某种原因取消他/她的订阅时，许可证密钥必须在当前计费周期结束之前有效。 由于用例并不复杂，我决定尽可能简单地解决它，使用可用的无服务器服务，遵循架构控制架构的原则。

无服务器调度程序

多年来，无服务器调度程序的主题一直相当频繁地出现。 在我看来，这是一个重复的问题，AWS 应该为我们提供托管解决方案。 幸运的是，AWS 提供了几个原语，您可以在构建自己的调度程序时使用它们。 最受欢迎的选项是：

  • 阶梯函数。

为业务问题选择解决方案

上述解决方案彼此之间存在显着差异。

首先，它们在指定呼叫时间和实际呼叫时间之间的时间段方面提供不同的操作精度（延迟） 。 例如，对于Dynamodb TTL，它可以长达 48 小时。 在 EventBridge 中使用 CRON 时，我们可以每分钟调用一次 Lambda 函数。 巨大的差异，对吧？

这是最重要的功能特性，因为它直接影响业务需求的实现。 在许多情况下，很难想象业务利益相关者会接受为期两天的暂停以响应用户操作。

我们可以描述这些解决方案的其他特征也很重要。 对于许多人来说，计划行动的最大数量与准确性一样重要。 另一个特点是未来推迟行动的最长时间。 当然，无论行动是周期性的还是一次性的。

更进一步，人们不能忘记运行成本和解决方案的复杂程度，直接影响实施时间。

考虑到所有这些特征，很快就会发现有很多选择，最终的解决方案取决于需求。

商业案例

它以订阅模式提供，只需支付少量月费或年费。 每个客户都有机会随时取消订阅。 在这种情况下，许可证密钥必须在当前计费周期结束之前有效，为此已收取费用。 看一下图中显示的t 1时间点。

该机制必须以类似方式适用于年度订阅。

解决方案的选择

鉴于 AWS 服务的业务需求和特性，我决定选择一个最容易实施和使用的解决方案。 这是一个经典的架构权衡，因为实现的简单性是以准确性为代价的。

在这种情况下，选择Dynamodb TTL（生存时间）机制被证明是最好的，因为：

  • 准确性（延迟）对我来说不是最重要的，在最坏的情况下，我的客户将免费获得额外 2 天的订阅时间😉
  • 我不需要循环调用 - 特定许可证只能取消一次
  • 实现起来很简单 - DynamoDB 表本身及其流就是您所需要的
  • 它符合事件驱动架构，AWS 会在未来自动触发预定的动作——推送而不是拉取的方式。
  • 允许您轻松检查将来要取消哪些许可证 - 只需查看 DynamoDB 表中的元素
  • 是最便宜的，尽管按照我的规模，每个解决方案都是免费的😉

解决方案的实施

该解决方案非常简单，由一个 Lambda 函数和一个带有流的 DynamoDB 表组成。

响应用户取消订阅，事件被发送到 EventBridge 中的eventBus 。 由于定义的规则，此事件被重定向到 Lambda 函数Scheduler （在实际解决方案中，其他组件也使用此事件）。 此元素（“记录”）具有基本结构，它包含允许您在Paidlicenses许可表中识别许可以及发生这种情况的时间的信息。

取消日期以Unix 时间格式保存在 DynamoDB 表配置中指定的属性下。 我将此属性称为ttl ，它在表的 CloudFormation 定义中定义，在第10行：

 SchedulingTable : Type : AWS::DynamoDB::Table Properties : AttributeDefinitions : - AttributeName : PK AttributeType : S KeySchema : - AttributeName : PK KeyType : HASH TimeToLiveSpecification : # ttl definition AttributeName : ttl Enabled : true BillingMode : PAY_PER_REQUEST TableName : Scheduling StreamSpecification : StreamViewType : OLD_IMAGE

使用 Node.js (JavaScript) 时，注意ttl计算。 它必须以秒而不是毫秒为单位提供。 因此，除以 1000：

 Math . floor ( new Date ( cancelationDateAsString ). getTime () / 1000 )

它是如何工作的

为了使整个解决方案有意义，我们必须对元素的删除事件做出反应。 我们使用触发DeactivatePaidLicense函数的流来执行此操作。 发送到此函数的有效负载包含先前存储在“Scheduling”表中的元素的所有数据，因此该函数通过在PaidLicenses表中进行适当的更新来知道要取消哪个许可证。

流和 Lambda 函数之间的连接在serverless.yml中定义：

 functions : deactivatePaidLicense : handler : src/deactivatePaidLicense/function.handler description : Deactivate license upon Paddle event events : - stream : type : dynamodb arn : !GetAtt SchedulingTable.StreamArn maximumRetryAttempts : 1 batchSize : 1 filterPatterns : - eventName : [ REMOVE ]

我在这里使用了一个过滤器，因此只有在从 table 中删除元素时才会调用该函数。 这样，我们将代码中的逻辑转移到 AWS 基础设施的配置中，这当然是最佳实践😃

笔记：

我这里没有使用单表设计方式，所以不用担心其他实体类型的移除。

解决方案在行动

我的粗略测试表明， us-east-1区域中的 DynamoDB 会在ttl属性中的指定时间后延迟 10 到 12 分钟删除元素。 它比上面提到的 48 小时限制要快得多，但对于许多解决方案来说仍然可能是不可接受的。 此外，我想强调的是，虽然这些是典型的延迟时间，但我们不能保证它们会一直这样。

我的观察与前段时间严崔进行的测试不谋而合。

总之，我必须得出结论，在实施上花费的最少时间提供了一个功能齐全的解决方案，可以满足我的业务需求并且易于操作。 这就是我的目标😃



原文:

When I was working on the license key management solution for my application CloudPouch I had to face the deferred cancelation of paid subscriptions problem.

When a user cancels his/her subscription for some reason, the license key must work until the end of the current billing period. Since the use case is not complicated, I decided to solve it as simple as possible, using the available Serverless services, following the principles of architecture-controlled architecture.

Serverless scheduler

The topic of the serverless scheduler has been appearing fairly regularly for years. In my opinion, this is a repetitive problem that AWS should provide us with a managed solution. Discussions in the AWS Community Builders Slack channel have not brought any effects, and we still need to implement it by ourselves.

Fortunately, AWS provides several primitives that you can use while building your own scheduler. The most popular options are:

  • DynamoDB TTL
  • CRON in EventBridge (used to be in CloudWatch)
  • StepFunctions.

Selection of a solution for a business problem

The solutions mentioned above differ significantly from each other.

Foremost, they offer different accuracy of operation (delay) in terms of the period between the designated and the actual time of calling. For example, for Dynamodb TTL it can be up to 48 hours. While using CRON in EventBridge, we can invoke a Lambda function every minute. Huge difference, right?

This is the most important, functional characteristic because it directly affects the implementation of business requirements. In numerous instances, it is difficult to imagine business stakeholders accepting a two-day hold-up in a response to a user action.

Other characteristics that we can describe these solutions are also important. For many, the maximum number of scheduled actions will be as important as accuracy. Another feature will be the maximum time to postpone the action in the future. And of course, whether the action is cyclical or one-off.

Going further, one cannot forget about the cost of running, and the level of complexity of the solution, directly affecting the time of implementation.

Taking all these characteristics into account, it quickly turns out that there are many options and the final solution depends on the requirements.

Business case

The CloudPouch tool is a desktop application for analysis and optimization of the AWS cloud costs. It is available in the subscription model, for a small monthly or annual fee. Each customer has the opportunity to cancel their subscription at any time. In such case, the license key must be valid until the end of the current billing period, for which the fee has already been charged. Take a look at t1 time point presented in the diagram.

The mechanism must work analogously for annual subscriptions.

Selection of the solution

Given the business requirements and the characteristics of AWS services, I decided to choose a solution that will be the easiest to implement and use. It was a classic architectural trade-off because the simplicity of implementation was obtained at the cost of accuracy.

Choosing the Dynamodb TTL (Time to Live) mechanism turned out to be the best in this case because:

  • Accuracy (delay) is not the most important for me, in the worst-case scenario my customer will receive 2 extra days of subscription for free 😉
  • I do not need cyclical calls - a particular license can be canceled only once
  • It's simple to implement - the DynamoDB table itself and its stream are all you need
  • It is in line with the Event-Driven Architecture, AWS will automatically trigger scheduled action in the future - push instead of pull approach.
  • Allows you to easily check which licenses are to be canceled in the future - just view the elements in the DynamoDB table
  • is the cheapest, although, with my scale, every solution would be free 😉

Implementation of the solution

The solution is very simple and consists of a Lambda function and a DynamoDB table with a stream.

In response to the cancelation of a subscription performed by the user, an event is sent to the eventBus in the EventBridge. Thanks to the defined rules, this event is redirected to the Lambda function Scheduler (in the real solution, other components consume this event as well). The Lambda function 'Scheduler' saves in the Scheduling table information about the license to be canceled. This element ("record") has a basic structure, it consists of information that allows you to identify the license in the table Paidlicenses and the time when this is going to happen.

The cancelation date is saved in the Unix time format under the attribute specified in the configuration of the DynamoDB table. I called this attribute ttl, it was defined in CloudFormation definition of the table, at line 10:

SchedulingTable: Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table Properties: AttributeDefinitions: - AttributeName: PK AttributeType: S KeySchema: - AttributeName: PK KeyType: HASH TimeToLiveSpecification: # ttl definition AttributeName: ttl Enabled: true BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST TableName: Scheduling StreamSpecification: StreamViewType: OLD_IMAGE

When using Node.js (JavaScript), pay attention to the ttl calculation. It must be provided in seconds and not milliseconds. Hence, the division by 1000:

Math.floor(new Date(cancelationDateAsString).getTime() / 1000)

How does it work

The AWS DynamoDB service constantly monitors our table and when the ttl value is older than the current time, it will delete the element.

For the whole solution to make sense, we must react to the deletion events of elements. We do it with a stream, which triggers the DeactivatePaidLicense function. The payload sent to this function contains all the data of the element that was previously stored in the 'Scheduling' table, thanks to which the function knows which license to cancel by making the appropriate update in the table PaidLicenses.

The connection between the stream and the Lambda function is defined in the serverless.yml:

functions: deactivatePaidLicense: handler: src/deactivatePaidLicense/function.handler description: Deactivate license upon Paddle event events: - stream: type: dynamodb arn: !GetAtt SchedulingTable.StreamArn maximumRetryAttempts: 1 batchSize: 1 filterPatterns: - eventName: [REMOVE]

I used a filter here, thanks to which the function will be called only as a result of removing the element from the table. In this way, we transfer logic from our code to the configuration of the AWS infrastructure, which is of course the best practice 😃

Note:

The Scheduling table is an independent table that only stores scheduled cancelations. I didn't use the single-table design approach here, so I don't have to worry about removals of other entity types.

Solution in action

My cursory tests have shown that the DynamoDB in the us-east-1 region deletes the elements with a delay of 10 to 12 minutes after a designated time in the ttl attribute. It is much faster than over mentioned 48 hours limit, but still may not be acceptable for many solutions. In addition, I want to highlight that while those are typical delay times, we have no guarantee that they will always be like that.

My observations coincide with tests carried out by Yan Cui some time ago.

In summary, I must conclude that minimal time spent on implementation delivered a fully functional solution that meets my business needs & is easy to operate. And that's what I was aiming for 😃

item.title: 开源冒险：第 58 集：PyScript：第一印象 | Open Source Adventures: Episode 58: PyScript: First Impressions
item.description:
让我们来看看。

文档大多都是错误的，所以有很多步骤可以让它运行起来。

下载

下载说明如下：

  • 立即下载 PyScript
  • 解压下载的文件
  • 复制您要使用的资产并将以下行添加到您的 html 文件中

只有一个问题，该 zip 中没有资产文件。

因此，我们需要手动下载https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css和https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js 。

然而这不起作用，因为它会在尝试获取pyscript.py时崩溃，所以我们也需要获取https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.py 。

本地服务器

我尝试创建一个 Hello World HTML 并将其作为本地文件打开，但这会导致即时 CORS 错误。

不幸的是，使用现代网络技术的任何事物都越来越普遍。

幸运的是 Python 自带了一个内置的 HTTP 服务器，所以我们可以运行python3 -m http.server 8080然后打开http://localhost:8080/hello.html

你好世界！

好的，让我们编写最简单的 Python 脚本：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset= "utf-8" /> <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1" /> <title> Hello World </title> <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "./pyscript.css" /> <script defer src= "./pyscript.js" ></script> </head> <body> <py-script> print("Hello World") </py-script> </body> </html>

如果从本地服务器打开，它至少不再在网络选项卡中出现任何错误。

然后会发生大约 10% 的时间我打印“Hello World”。 并且 90% 的时间在控制台中都会出现这样的错误：

 JsException: SyntaxError: Failed to execute 'querySelector' on 'Document': '#-49bea52c-4893-412d-cba1-447d24c65f0a' is not a valid selector.

并且文档中只有一个粉色条。 我认为这可能是某些 Chrome 扩展的问题，所以我在隐身窗口或 Safari 中尝试了它，同样的事情。

这很明显是一些比赛条件。

你应该使用 PyScript 吗？

显然还没有。

接下来

所有代码都在 GitHub 上。

我想在某个时候回到 PyScript，但在下一集中，我们将真正了解 Opal Ruby，它最近发布了 1.5 版本。



原文:

An alpha version of PyScript just came out, with tagline "Run Python in Your HTML". Let's check it out.

Documentation is mostly all wrong, so there's a lot of steps to get it even running.

Download

The download instructions are:

  • Download PyScript now
  • Unzip the downloaded file
  • Copy the assets you want to use and add the following lines to your html file

There's just one problem, there are no asset files in that zip.

So instead we need to manually download https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.css and https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.js instead.

That however does not work, as it then crashes trying to get pyscript.py, so we need to get https://pyscript.net/alpha/pyscript.py as well.

Local server

I tried just creating a Hello World HTML and opening it as a local file, but that got into instant CORS error.

It's unfortunately more and more common with anything that uses modern web technologies.

Fortunately Python comes with a builtin HTTP server, so we can run python3 -m http.server 8080 and then open http://localhost:8080/hello.html

Hello, World!

OK, with that out of the way, let's write the simplest possible Python script:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head> <meta charset="utf-8" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" /> <title>Hello World</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="./pyscript.css" /> <script defer src="./pyscript.js"></script>
</head>
<body> <py-script> print("Hello World") </py-script>
</body>
</html>

If opened from local server, it at least no longer gets any errors in the network tab.

What happens then is that about 10% of time I get "Hello World" printed. And 90% of time there's an error in console like this:

JsException: SyntaxError: Failed to execute 'querySelector' on 'Document': '#-49bea52c-4893-412d-cba1-447d24c65f0a' is not a valid selector.

And only a pink bar in the document. I thought it might be some issues with some Chrome Extensions, so I tried it in an incognito window or in Safari, same thing.

It's very clearly some race condition.

Should you use PyScript?

Obviously not yet.

Coming next

All the code is on GitHub.

I want to come back to PyScript at some point, but in the next episode we'll actually take a look at Opal Ruby, which recently got 1.5 release.
item.author: taw

item.title: 如何在 Yandex、Google Chrome、Mozilla FireFox、Opera 和 Microsoft Edge 中查看保存的密码 | How to View Saved Passwords in Yandex, Google Chrome, Mozilla FireFox, Opera and Microsoft Edge
item.description:
阅读有关在 PC 或移动设备上的浏览器中查看用户帐户密码的信息，其中包含针对前 5 种浏览器的特定指南。 任何现代浏览器都具有保存密码的功能。 它为您省去了每次都必须输入注册数据的麻烦——很少有人能记住他们最喜欢的网站和在线服务的数百个登录名和密码。

但是，如果自动填充由于某种原因不起作用，并且您不记得密码或登录名，您该怎么办？ 另一种情况是当您想从浏览器中删除所有自动填充的数据时，例如登录名、密码、地址和银行详细信息，以确保其他人看不到它们。

让我们了解如何在 Google Chrome、Opera、Yandex.Browser、Mozilla Firefox、Microsoft Edge 或其他浏览器中查看或删除已保存的密码。 您还将学习如何为登录名和地址以及银行卡详细信息设置自动填写。

谷歌浏览器

我们从谷歌浏览器开始。 要查看浏览器保存的所有密码：

转到菜单/设置/高级/密码和表格/管理密码

此菜单包含您的浏览器以网站 - 用户名 - 密码格式保存的所有密码。

默认情况下，密码是隐藏的。 单击眼睛形按钮以查看密码。

但是，只有启用此功能才能看到它。 默认情况下，所有浏览器都开启了此功能。 如果您不想保存您的密码和登录名 - 禁用此功能并每次手动输入登录名和密码。

要删除已保存的密码，请单击密码旁边带有三个垂直点的菜单按钮，然后选择“删除”。

如果您需要删除有关已保存密码的所有数据，您可以从清除浏览历史记录的菜单中执行此操作。 为此，请转到 Google Chrome 菜单并选择历史记录/历史记录/清除数据。 选中密码框，然后单击“清除数据”。

如果您使用 Chrome Sync 和 Google 帐户，您的所有登录名和密码也会自动同步到您的 Google 帐户。 您可以在您的 Google 帐户页面 - https://passwords.google.com中查看它们。

它们从您的计算机和 Android 设备同步。

要查看 Yandex.Browser 中保存的密码：

转到菜单/设置/设置/密码和表格/管理密码。

此菜单包含您的浏览器以网站 - 用户名 - 密码格式保存的所有密码。

默认情况下，密码是隐藏的。 要查看它，请单击它并选择显示。

有时，系统可能会要求您提供帐户密码。 在这种情况下，输入它。

但是，只有启用此功能，您才能看到密码。

要删除 Yandex.Browser 中所有保存的密码，请转到浏览数据清除菜单，选择“保存的密码”，然后单击“清除浏览数据”。

阅读全文以及所有其他视频教程。 也访问我们的Youtube 频道，有超过 400 个视频教程。



原文:

Read about viewing user account passwords in their browser on a PC or mobile gadget, with particular guides for every of the top 5 browsers. Any modern browser has a function to save passwords. It saves you the trouble of having to enter your registration data every time – few people can remember hundreds of logins and passwords to their favorite websites and online services.

But what can you do if the autofill doesn’t work for some reason, and you don’t remember the password or login? Another situation is when you want to delete all auto filled data from your browser, such as logins, passwords, addresses and banking details to make sure that other people don’t see them.

Let’s find out how to see or delete saved passwords in Google Chrome, Opera, Yandex.Browser, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge or other browsers. You will also learn how to set up auto filling for logins and addresses as well as banking card details.

YouTube:

Google Chrome

We begin with Google Chrome. To see all passwords saved by your browser:

Go to Menu / Settings / Advanced / Passwords and forms / Manage passwords

This menu contains all passwords saved by your browser in the format Website – Username – Password.

By default, the password is hidden. Click on the eye-shaped button to see the password.

However, you will be able to see it only if this function is enabled. By default, all browsers have this function on. If you don’t want your passwords and logins saved – disable this function and enter logins and passwords manually every time.

To delete a saved password, click on the menu button with the three vertical dots next to the password, and select “Remove.”

If you need to delete all data about saved passwords, you can do it from the menu for clearing browsing history. To do it, go to Google Chrome menu and select History / History / Clear data. Check the Passwords box and click on “Clear data.”

If you use Chrome Sync and Google account, all your logins and passwords are also synchronized automatically to your Google account. You can view them all in your Google Account page – https://passwords.google.com.

They are synchronized both from your computer and Android devices.

Yandex.Browser

To view saved passwords in Yandex.Browser:

Go to Menu / Settings / Settings / Passwords and forms / Manage passwords.

This menu contains all passwords saved by your browser in the format Website – Username – Password.

By default, the password is hidden. To view it, click on it and choose Show.

Sometimes, the system may ask for your account password. In this case, enter it.

However, you will be able to see passwords only if this function is enabled.

To delete all saved passwords in Yandex.Browser, go to the browsing data clearing menu, select “Saved passwords” and click on “Clear browsing data.”

Read the full article with all additional video tutorials. Also visit our Youtube channel, there are over 400 video tutorials.

item.title: 颤振覆盖加载器 | Flutter Overlay loader
item.description:
沙波兰

发表于一个简单的 Flutter 包，用于在获取远程数据或任何长时间运行的异步任务时管理加载程序。 您只需编写两行代码即可显示加载程序。

不覆盖 Appbar 的覆盖加载器

入门

安装

dependencies : flutter_overlay_loader : ^1.0.8

进口

import 'package:flutter_overlay_loader/flutter_overlay_loader.dart' ;

如何使用

当开始网络调用时，然后调用这行代码..

 Loader . show ( context , progressIndicator: LinearProgressIndicator ());

完成网络调用后，调用：

您可以自定义此加载程序..

 Loader . show ( context , isSafeAreaOverlay: false , isBottomBarOverlay: false , overlayFromBottom: 80 , overlayColor: Colors . black26 , progressIndicator: CircularProgressIndicator ( backgroundColor: Colors . red ), themeData: Theme . of ( context ) . copyWith ( colorScheme: ColorScheme . fromSwatch (). copyWith ( secondary: Colors . green )) );

您还可以使用该属性检查加载程序是否显示：

 and you can prevent back pressed like this snippet: ```dart WillPopScope( child: //TODO , onWillPop:()async => !Loader.isShown )

最后在 dispose 方法上调用 hide 方法：

 @override void dispose ( ) { Loader . hide (); super . dispose (); }

Martin Jablečník - Martin Jablečník - Dec 4 '21 21 年 12 月 4 日



原文:

Shah Poran

Posted on May 14

A simple Flutter package for managing loader when fetching remote data or any long running async task. Flutter overlay loader is easy to use.

You can show loader using only write two lines of code.

Overlay loader without overlaying Appbar

Overlay Loader without overlaying BottomAppBar and also overlaying AppBar

Getting Started

Installing 

dependencies: flutter_overlay_loader: ^1.0.8

Import 

import 'package:flutter_overlay_loader/flutter_overlay_loader.dart';

How To Use

When start network call then call this line of code..

Loader.show(context,progressIndicator:LinearProgressIndicator());

After finished network call then call :

You can customize this loader..

Loader.show(context, isSafeAreaOverlay: false, isBottomBarOverlay: false, overlayFromBottom: 80, overlayColor: Colors.black26, progressIndicator: CircularProgressIndicator(backgroundColor: Colors.red), themeData: Theme.of(context) .copyWith(colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSwatch().copyWith(secondary: Colors.green))
);

You can also check loader is showing or not using the property :


 and you can
prevent back pressed like this snippet:



```dart
WillPopScope(
  child: //TODO , 
  onWillPop:()async => !Loader.isShown
)

Finally dispose call hide method on dispose method :

@override
void dispose() { Loader.hide(); super.dispose();
}
